subject: New message from LiAnn Yim's 2022 – Grade 11 – Denlow – New Orleans class
body_text:
For our last evening in New Orleans, we enjoyed a breezy ride on the St. Charles streetcar. This is the oldest continuously operating streetcar line in the world, in operation since 1835. (Fun fact: the St. Charles streetcar line is one of only two "moving" streetcar National Historic Landmarks. The other is the San Francisco cable car system!) We took it from the Central Business District (CBD) to the Garden District (where Tulane is located) and got dinner at Dat Dog, a New Orleans culinary institution that serves up gourmet sausages and hot dogs. We followed this up with ice cream from Creole Creamery, another staple in New Orleans. This ice cream parlor makes its artisan ice creams and sorbets in-house every day. 

This was such a lovely way to end a packed day—from learning about the environmental challenges facing southern Louisiana at the Pontchartrain Conservancy this morning, to seeing this unique natural landscape from the water this afternoon. 

If you'd like to see more photos from our New Orleans trip, you can view and download all of them here: rmd.me/GnpUYKewBoY rmd.me/k145Lkdc1HK

subject: Word of the Day: Synecdoche
body_text:
👋 Welcome to WordDaily, the quickest way to learn a new word every day!

https://track.worddaily.com/?xtl=xdk8j5xt4i1v69lqhoua7m0kchrttq3l4ux0z3if34tcometc4qnxtrsuxw3twydnpbt00cp87c7cksv3mxg76mizd2ajp0fdl2y9grp6ofb8&eih=rgu53zsphz58u1kwls7qkt2ptbrc4vayo14&__stmp=senl62

Monday, November 1, 2022

https://track.worddaily.com/?xtl=xdk8j5xt4i1v69lqhoua7m0kchrttq3l4x9bildigozaox8cwbmuj834393xkuiurtbad43acjisnfb06msx5gk5bidrppjn9qi7aad99ehtj&eih=rgu53zsphz58u1kwls7qkt2ptbrc4vayo14&__stmp=senl62

https://track.worddaily.com/?xtl=xdk8j5xt4i1v69lqhoua7m0kchrttq3l4zkr6hz0xwfyohalrm6c8kcdziz4rt69vxbiu7t09a2aiajt9mno4vxqe2s5fqfecgbf3v35p8j93&eih=rgu53zsphz58u1kwls7qkt2ptbrc4vayo14&__stmp=senl62

jocund

joc•und

 

Noun1. person's name, especially a nickname or alias

 

2. Morbi interdum mi bibendum libero imperdiet, eget volutpat diam aliquet. Maecenas auctor luctus risus ut hendrerit.
AdverbSuspendisse vulputate nibh mi, ac sagittis dui venenatis a.

 

https://track.worddaily.com/?xtl=1ox7haikitkheip3jcg6na2bgirww9o5oamuu6oy7evq47hk5l7fv1oet6tgh4f96id2qkj3c5ks6te6zgys4vf5p6oltrz64xroehk9wc2kzufn79iz3vl0j12v54jom5wpzxo3iwrumewnvvud5vh1yjrqchjkdnqe46809b58hja5ifzxnpc6avbcg0yfj81spgvtkf4rm6cfzq3yzt5&eih=rgu53zsphz58u1kwls7qkt2ptbrc4vayo14&__stmp=senl62&email=scottmsilver%40gmail.com

WordDaily remains free for everyone thanks to our advertisers.

EXAMPLE SENTENCES

“I think “Happy” is an appropriate moniker for someone who smiles so much.”

 

Word Origin

 

1849: said to be originally a hobo term (but monekeer is attested in London underclass from 1851), of uncertain origin; perhaps from monk (monks and nuns take new names with their vows, and early 19c.

 

Why This Word?

subject: Important Info for Madrichim Attending Retreat
body_text:
Shalom Madrichim & Parents,


We had another fantastic site visit at Hidden Villa yesterday, and we are
now ready to share more details about the Retreat 🙂

In this email, you will find:

   1.

   Schedule of Activities
   <https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Q01PKE47WsACbyLX17IU_c_UkIrok7dQW67KCCLmEjY/edit?usp=sharing>
(includes
   drop-off and pick-up directions)
   2.

   Packing List
   <https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fF_3zEKJsUpAW6Nr9xQifJNnz1KndH86amjswMWhNIA/edit?usp=sharing>
   3.

   Leadership Role Sign-up Sheet
   <https://docs.google.com/document/d/1JK6l7jprFZn2_FrB3Qz9JhWdJwh6iGCcnDhHbkHErAU/edit?usp=sharing>
   (all Madrichim on the Retreat must sign up)
   4.

   Permission Form to Administer Medications during the Retreat
   <https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSf8qKg2OeYONQ-igo965mkorE6BdWEVu3WwLLsCt6B4uxtn3Q/viewform?usp=sf_link>
   (must be completed by a parent)

This is also a reminder that parents must complete these tasks before the
Retreat:

   1.

   Madrichim Registration
   <https://kolemeth.shulcloud.com/form/23-24-kennections-registration.html>
   2.

   If you have not completed our Emergency Form or if your info has
   changed, please click here
   <https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSe-7dxEBiTOwrLs8q_mNxy2R6oyFsRb9eGmcyPJWx_7Detixw/viewform?usp=sf_link>
   .
   3.

   If you did not receive or sign our digital parent waivers, please email
   me.

Feel free to reach out with any questions. As always, meals will include
gluten-free and dairy-free options. All food and snacks will be kosher (no
meat) and nut/peanut-free.


Shabbat Shalom!
-- 
*Maytal Miller*
*Assistant Director of **Educational Programming*
Congregation Kol Emeth
4175 Manuela Avenue
Palo Alto, CA 94306
650-948-7498

-- 
To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send an email to KolEmethMadrichim+unsubscribe@kolemeth.org.

subject: Baruch Ha'ba to Mike Dolinka
body_text: Meet our new Director of Development Dear Scott, We are excited to announce that after an intensive search process, Mike Dolinka begins this week as our new Director of Development. As valued members of our donor community, we wanted to share this with you ahead of tomorrow's announcement in the Ruach Report monthly newsletter. Todah rabbah (thank you!) for your partnership, support, and dedication to Camp Ramah in California. Originally from San Diego, Mike has a rich personal connection to Ramah, where he began as a camper (Machon 2000) before working as a madrich (counselor) for several years, and staffing Ramah Seminar in Israel in 2006. He then joined the IDF Nachal Brigade as a chayal boded (lone soldier) alongside several longtime Ramah friends. Mike lived in Israel for several years after his IDF service before returning to the US. Mike brings diverse professional experience to Camp Ramah, having worked in the technology and healthcare industries as well as in Jewish nonprofit management. Mike previously served as Assistant Director for the Anti-Defamation League in San Diego, Associate Director for AIPAC in San Diego and Desert Communities, and Executive Director for the Friends of the Israel Defense Forces in San Diego, Orange County, and Arizona. Following his time in the nonprofit sector, Mike attained his MBA and co-founded a veterinary technology company, serving as the Director of Strategic Accounts. Most recently, Mike was a Director at DaVita, overseeing business development in its Southern California hospital services group and operations for the Cedars-Sinai Health System. Mike resides in the Pico Robertson area with his wife Lauren (whom he met in Israel and who is currently Lower School Director at Sinai Akiba Academy), their son Lev, and their dog, Buddha. Mike is excited about the opportunity to reconnect with his roots at Camp Ramah and is dedicated to ensuring its continued success. He deeply understands Ramah’s profound impact on Jewish youth and the future of the Jewish community and is looking forward to working with the incredible Ramah mishpacha (family). We are eager to begin working with Mike as we look ahead towards our 70th anniversary in 2026 and imagine what the next seventy years will hold for Ramah. In the coming days and weeks, Mike will begin getting to know our amazing, dedicated kehillah (community). Please feel free to reach out to introduce yourself or arrange a time to connect; his email is mike@ramah.org. B'shalom, Lee S. Rosen, MD Chair, Board of Directors Nancy Beiser Development Chair Rabbi Joe Menashe Executive Director Mike with tzevet (staff) friends on the givah (hill), circa 2003.     Camp Ramah in California 17525 Ventura Blvd., #310 Encino, CA 91316 SUPPORT RAMAH   Camp Ramah in California | 17525 Ventura Boulevard, Suite 310, Encino, CA 91316 Unsubscribe scott.m.silver@alum.dartmouth.org Update Profile | Constant Contact Data Notice Sent by rabbijoe@ramah.org powered by Try email marketing for free today!

>
>
> We'd like to introduce you to Sofia, who just arrived from Ukraine with
> her family, as they fled the war.
> [image: save-suri-banner]
> <http://newsletters.worldisraelnews.com/c/38112?id=632949644.90460.1.f11e8ab02e0b52affe761abeabe6e2bb>
>
> This is Ilan from Israel Children Cancer Centers, I hope all is well.
>
>
>
> I am sure by now you are well familiar with the great work that we do when
> it comes to helping children diagnosed with complex medical conditions.
>
> I just wanted to reach out to you directly as we were recently introduced
> to Sofia, who just arrived from Ukraine with her family, as they fled the
> war. Sofia urgently needs complex surgery on a rapidly developing bone
> tumor, and her family has no resources to cover the costs.
>
>
>
> Sofia is approved for an operation at an Israeli hospital, known to have
> one of the best oncology departments in the world. You have been
> instrumental in the past, so we are reaching out again to please help us
> raise the necessary funds to help save Sofia's life.

> battling cancer, heart conditions and other dangerous diseases. Please

>

>

subject: Alex!
body_text:
Alex -

Though he is an a-hole, the kid really does have talent and a required
stubborn interest in the general programming, programming thinking area.  I
say "required stubborn interest" because I view programming as a series of
frustrating speed bumps and dead ends while you fail your way to getting
something to work.

We left a relatively difficult exercise about 50% done last night. I'd like
to see if he'll make the time to finish it completely. No worries if he
doesn't, but there is a last key skill to develop which is to be frustrated
with just himself and the computer and no one nearby.

Scott

subject: Re: shared equity agreement
body_text:
Thanks Scott, I just have a few questions:

1) Should there be some kind of buyout clause or mechanism? For instance if
we wanted to buy out your $300k share at some future point

2) Any clarification we should add in the awful event that a trustee passes
on or wants to transfer their share to a child?

3) Any need to be explicit on refinancing without prior notice from
trustees? Something like "The SMITHS may refinance the Property without
requiring the consent of the TRUSTEES, provided that the refinancing does
not encumber the TRUSTEES’ Ownership Percentage or diminish their proceeds
on sale.”

4) Maybe just a clarifying question on "1.4: The Trustees’ Ownership
Percentage"
imagine SMITHS put $500k into improving the house say 5 years from now
Then it's sold for $4.5M in 10 years
Am i correct that the Trustee's ownership percentage in this case would be
100 * (300,000 / (2,190,000 + 500,000) = 11.2% ?

($2.19M was the purchase price)





On Thu, Apr 3, 2025 at 9:18 PM Scott Silver <scottmsilver@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi there both -
>
> The next step is to formalize our investment in your house.
> Here is a blank version of the agreement we'd ask you to sign.
>
>
> https://docs.google.com/document/d/1sJzOAb0ZN9HpusQqPSxMoBUTY3iBdB4DE1-YywGZfCU/edit?tab=t.0
>
> Would you let me know if you have any questions or concerns? If not, I
> will get the lawyer working on it.
>
> Thanks,
>
> Scott
>

subject: Re: fundraising meeting is Friday 11/21 @ 830am
body_text:
I'll parlay December's gratitude exercise into the 2025 fundraising
operation. The thanks/ask cycle hearkens back to the preso you put together
with Lisa and Rachel several months ago. It's a new norm for the Board.

Talk Sunday!

Safe travels.

~Russ

On Fri, Nov 22, 2024, 5:38 AM Scott Silver <scottmsilver@gmail.com> wrote:

> 12PT works
>
> I think on the KE fundraising thing for Kol Nidrei, etc, I think David was
> right that Dale's talking to everyone is a thing we need to be good at.
> I think it would be *amazing* if we got good at reaching out to everyone a
> few times a year and made at least one or 2 of those reach outs about
> fundraising. My parent's loved being called (and saying no).
> Conceptually the idea would be to operationalize "Call everyone" And this
> becomes a board task (sharded by number of board members)
>
> Thanks,
>
> Scott
>
> On Thu, Nov 21, 2024 at 8:05 PM Russell Gottfried <
> russellgottfried@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> Well, certainly enjoy your time there. Shall we talk ~noon PST on Sunday?
>> Let me know if there's another time that's better for you.
>>
>> I hope tomorrow's fundraising meeting is productive.
>>
>> You made excellent points last night regarding the emphasis on governance
>> and on the Operating Budget major donor gap. Next quarter, we'll have
>> forecasts so we'll see how the shortfall propagates. My sense is that FYQ3
>> (Jan-Mar) is going to be extremely lean. I'm in the process of getting
>> Rachel some reinforcements for fundraising.
>>
>> Have a good night!
>>
>> On Wed, Nov 20, 2024 at 11:59 AM Scott Silver <scottmsilver@gmail.com>
>> wrote:
>>
>>> I’d love to on Sunday, but I’ll be in Park City. So probably has to be
>>> VC.
>>>
>>>
>>> Acknowledged re:David and alternating.
>>>
>>> On Wed, Nov 20, 2024 at 11:46 AM Russell Gottfried <
>>> russellgottfried@gmail.com> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Re: David, I recommend alternating Tuesday evening and Friday morning
>>>> meetings so only half of us will be out of the loop at anytime. You do
>>>> double duty, of course, but it's still only on,e meeting a week and you
>>>> could readily cut them to half-hour sessions. You can even cancel by fiat
>>>> as needed... Also, maybe consider assigning a rotating note taker in chief.
>>>> Or get Gemini to do it ;-p Food for thought.
>>>>
>>>> Since both of us are relatively unprogrammed on Sunday, are you
>>>> interested in connecting over a cup of coffee or a beer? Of course, phone
>>>> or Meet work just as well...
>>>>
>>>> Have a great afternoon. See you tonight on zoom which, at least, is
>>>> better than Teams.
>>>>
>>>> On Wed, Nov 20, 2024, 9:31 AM Scott Silver <scottmsilver@gmail.com>
>>>> wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> Ok, maybe that time won't work. Trying accommodate David is hard on
>>>>> times, but he is also critical to fundraising at the moment. That is a
>>>>> thing we must fix.
>>>>>
>>>>> Excellent re Dubinsky. Great work.
>>>>> Sunday, yes will work. remind what time works? I'm basically doing
>>>>> nothing.
>>>>>
>>>>> Scott
>>>>>
>>>>> On Tue, Nov 19, 2024 at 8:16 PM Russell Gottfried <
>>>>> russellgottfried@gmail.com> wrote:
>>>>>
>>>>>> Hi Scott,
>>>>>>
>>>>>> I won't be able to make the Friday morning meeting (and maybe not the
>>>>>> time slot in general). A couple of quick updates
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    - met w Krigel: I asked for $50k, but Mike said it was too steep,
>>>>>>    especially due to annual giving. I'll follow-up: we should divert him to
>>>>>>    Building only. If so, I think he'll give $50k (over four years). I'll ask
>>>>>>    again in January.
>>>>>>    - Saper passed (for now): I think they'll give eventually -- will
>>>>>>    ask again in the new year. Jeff thought other Wilson Sonsini alums (Ron
>>>>>>    Roth and David Steuer) would give. I don't know Ron, but will contact David.
>>>>>>    - Donna Dubinsky: you may have seen my update in the spreadsheet
>>>>>>    -- they gave $100k which will arrive in December; they were excited about
>>>>>>    the matching gift and prefer that this gift pay down the principal.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> I'll reach out to Eddie Gornish and Len Lehmann after Thanksgiving.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> If you are still available on Sunday, let me know what timing would
>>>>>> work for me to call.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Have a good night. See you online tomorrow at the Board meeting.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> ~Russ.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> On Tue, Nov 19, 2024 at 2:31 PM Scott Silver <scottmsilver@gmail.com>
>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>
>>>>>>> FYI -
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> By agreement we moved our Fundraising meeting this week to 830am on
>>>>>>> Friday. This will be a trial to see if this can work.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Tx,
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Scott
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>

subject: ⏳ Last Chance to Join the Honorary Committee
body_text: Email from Camp Ramah in California Join us in honoring Rabbi Ed Feinstein & Rabbi Nina Bieber Feinstein, and the Miller Family Grandchildren Dear Scott, We are about to send our 2024 Gala Celebration invitation to the printer, and we wanted to give you one last opportunity to join the honorary committee and be listed as a valued supporter of Camp Ramah in California. This year's gala will honor Rabbi Ed Feinstein & Rabbi Nina Bieber Feinstein, along with the Miller family grandchildren, who are the recipients of the Judith Weinstock Alumni Leadership Award. Your participation in the honorary committee helps us celebrate these incredible honorees and supports the growth and impact of our programs. If you’d like to be included, please let us know by the end of this week. (Scroll down for the current honorary committee list.) Thank you for being an important part of the Ramah community and G'mar Chatima Tova, Nancy Beiser & Lesley Wolman Gala Celebration Co-Chairs Click here to join the Honorary Committee Click here for Sponsorships, Tributes, and Tickets Sunday, December 15, 2024 Valley Beth Shalom 5:30PM Cocktail Reception with Hearty Hors D'oeuvres 7:00PM ﻿Program Help us to honor the Feinstein and Miller families by joining the honorary committee and by purchasing a sponsorship, tribute, or tickets. Click here to join the Honorary Committee Click here for Sponsorships, Tributes, and Tickets For more information, please contact Mike Dolinka, Director of Development, at 858-888-5494 or mike@ramah.org ﻿We look forward to seeing you on Sunday, December 15, 2024! subject: Notification of Cases - Week Ending February 13, 2022
body_text:
[1] View this email in your browser

Key Information

There were 6 positive cases reported (2 at San Mateo and 4 at Hillsborough campus) for the week ending February 13

We will be offering testing options during February break. See below for more information.

Dear Nueva Community,

The health and safety of our students and staff is our top priority. This letter is to inform you that 6 community members(2 at San Mateo and 4 at Hillsborough) tested positivefor COVID-19 last week. Consistent with the SMPH and SMCOE [2] Pandemic Recovery Frameworkprotocols, all positive individuals are isolating, under the care of their healthcare providers, and will not return to school until they have completed their isolations and resolved symptoms. Those who may have been exposed have been notified. If you have not been specifically contacted, no further action is required on your part.

Thank you for your continued mindfulness to help our community remain healthy and safe.  Please continue to:

Complete the daily health screening and stay home if symptomatic for any reason

Mask (considering updated federal, state, and county recommendations to useN95, KN95, or double mask with surgical under cloth masks, which are available at school)

Distance, when possible

Ventilate indoors or outdoors

Refrain from non-essential gatherings

Vaccinate and boost, if eligible

Comply with our required weekly PCR surveillance testing and any recommended supplemental testing

Regarding testing: All students, faculty, and staff are required to test onsite on regular testing days—Wednesday (Hillsborough Campus), Thursday (San Mateo campus), or Friday (make-up testing on the San Mateo campus for community members of either campus)—or submit negative offsite PCR tests the week prior to returning onsite each week.

Onsite Testing During February Break

All community members will need to submit a PCR COVID negative test prior to returning onsite following winter break, and we will offer onsite testing options during this break. Registration is required and can be done using [3] this link.

Wednesday, Feb. 23, 8 a.m.–12 p.m., Hillsborough campus, GCC
Thursday, Feb. 24, 8 a.m.–12 p.m., San Mateo campus, 175A
Friday, Feb. 25, 8 a.m.–10 a.m., San Mateo campus, 175A

All PCR tests must be taken—and results provided—between Monday and Sunday prior to return the following Monday. As always, onsite testing results will be automatically reported to us. [4] Offsite PCR tests must be submitted here(please do NOT email results to [5] nurse@nuevaschool.org).

Please contact [6] nurse@nuevaschool.orgor your healthcare provider if you have any questions. And thank you for your support!

Sincerely,

The Nueva Health Team

Important Resources:

Submit [7] offsite test results

Submit [8] student vaccination updates

Submit [9] employee/contractor vaccination updates

Submit [10] parent/guardian/visitor vaccination updates

Find [11] testing and case dashboard

Find [12] general community letters

Alger Digital Trust & Safety // Founder, The Vault by Matty (https://www.linkedin.com/comm/in/matthew-e-a-899139230?midToken=AQHFlpp0n_Q0Zw&midSig=1OEqNq6xgpfqU1&trk=eml-email_lss_inmail_reminder_02-inmail-1-view_profile&trkEmail=eml-email_lss_inmail_reminder_02-inmail-1-view_profile-null-9roc%7Ell6qkjfq%7Evn-null-neptune%2Fprofile%7Evanity%2Eview&lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_lss_inmail_reminder_02%3BZ5gpJ6%2B6SCqgXLE3tC1fmw%3D%3D)
------------------
"Hi Scott,

I work for Sift. Some of our clients are Twitter and LinkedIn- we are a fraud prevention platform that utilizes custom machine learning and gathers from a global data network to prevent fraud in real time.

I wanted to ask how you’re handling fraud and if you are open to having an overview discussion to see how Sift’s platform aligns with YouTube's initiatives?

Let me know a few days that work for you in the next week or two and I’ll be glad to accommodate your schedule.

Thanks, Scott.


Matthew Alger"
------------------

Yes, interested:

subject: Amex Points for travel to India (have enuf for Biz class for Jules/Joy)
body_text:
* FLIGHTS FOR JOY AND JULES *

Scott - I want to use amex points to fly business class for Joy/Jules from
SFO to Dubai and back on Air Emirates.

Jules and I are flying SFO to Dubai and then Jaipur outbound.
Jules and I are flying Delhi to Dubai and then SFO return.

See attached screenshot of the JUST SFO To DUBAI and DUBAI to SFO Emirates
flights. These are the flights I want to use Amex points for in biz class.

I can then ask Santiago can ticket me normally from Dubai to Jaipur and
from Delhi to Dubai on the return to meet up with these Air Emirates
flights.

For you - You will fly to Delhi however Becky gets you there. You will join
us in Jaipur.
Then, We will need to ticket you from Delihi to Dubai to SFO. It will be
cheaper if Becky books your ticket roundtrip from SFO to Delhi on your own
and then back from Delhi via Dubai with Jules/Joy.
Can you please login to Amex and see if this is possible?
Thanks.
Love,
Joy


*From San Francisco to Dubai, with Emirates*

   -
   - Leave San Francisco on Friday, April 5, at *5:05 pm*
   - Fly 15 hours and 35 minutes with *Emirates 226*
   - Emirates operates the flight with an Airbus 380 aircraft
   <https://www.emirates.com/english/experience/our-fleet/the-new-emirates-a380/>
(two
   decks)
   - In all cases, each of you can check 2 pieces of luggage
   - Each piece can weigh 50 or 70 pounds depending on the class of service
   - The tickets are non-refundable




*Layover in Dubai (switch airports)*

   - Arrive on Saturday, April *6* at 7:40 pm (*Dubai* airport)
   - Depart on Saturday, April *6 *at 11:25 pm (*Sharjah* airport nearby)
   - The layover in Dubai is *3 hours and 45 minutes*
   - Skip the line at immigration and customs at the Dubai airport
   - American passport holders do *NOT* need a visa to travel to United
   Arab Emirates
   - Collect your checked luggage
   - Travel 15 miles (20 to 40 minutes) to the *Sharjah International
   Airport*
   - Check in with the low-cost airline Air Arabia
   <https://www.airarabia.com/en>
   - Access a dedicated check-in counter and skip the lines at
   immigration/security (USD 37.00 per person)


*From Dubai to Jaipur*

   - Leave Dubai at *11:25 pm*
   - Fly 3 hours and 10 minutes with *Air Arabia* flight 9435
   - This flight is operated with an Airbus 320 aircraft equipped with economy
   class
   <https://cdn.businesstraveller.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/11/Air-Arabia-2.jpg>
    only
   - The seat map
   <https://cdn.seatguru.com/en_US/img/20231129133323/seatguru/airlines_new/Air_Arabia/Air_Arabia_Airbus_A320.svg>
is
   ABC-DEF, from row 1 to 29
   - Seats with extra leg room (row 12, emergency exit) are available at no
   additional cost
   - Arrive in *Jaipur *India on Sunday, April *7*, at *4:05 am*
   - Each of you can check 66 pounds of lugagge
   - The tickets are refundable up to 8 hours prior to departure
   - The airfare is 282.00 American Dollars per person
   - Air Arabia processes payments in United Arab Emirates Dirham
   - Air Arabia accepts *Visa* and *Mastercard*
   - The passenger must be able to show the credit card used for the
   payment upon check-in at the airport (in Dubai)

Return Flights:

   - Leave Delhi on Sunday, April 14, at *4:15 am*
   - Fly 3 hours and 35 minutes, Emirates flight 513
   - Change planes in Dubai (remain in transit, same terminal)
   - The layover in Dubai is 2 hours and 50 minutes
   - Fly 15 hours and 50 minutes, Emirates flight 225
   - Arrive in San Francisco on Sunday, April 14, at *2:00 pm*



   -
   - The *ONE-WAY* airfares *for Scott* are:
      - In economy class: 1,201.60 Canadian Dollars (*USD 890.00*)
      - In premium economy: 2,268.40 Canadian Dollars (*USD 1,681.00* )
      - In business class: 3,702.00 Canadian Dollars (*USD 2,742.00* )


When the return flights (from Delhi to San Francisco) *with Emirates* presented
above are *combined* with the outbound Emirates flight for *Joy and
Jules* (from
San Francisco to Dubai) the *ROUND-TRIP* airfares are these:


   - In economy class: 1,796.80 Canadian Dollars (*USD 1,332.00*) *per
   person*
   - In premium economy: 4,638.80 Canadian Dollars (*USD 3,436.00*) *per
   person*
   - In business class: 8,849.60 Canadian Dollars (*USD 6,556.00*) *per
   person*

Scott,

We are committed to keeping you informed of changes to your banking experience that may affect you. Beginning Monday, April 8, construction will occur on the sidewalks of our San Jose location, at 181 East Tasman Drive, Suite 10, San Jose, CA 95134. We anticipate construction will be completed within two weeks.

While this construction is in progress, you will not be able to use the main entrance of our location, although the back entrance will be available. You can access the back entrance through the attached parking garage where there will be signs to direct you to the service hallway.

We apologize for any inconvenience this may cause. If you would like to skip in-person visits during this time, we encourage you to schedule a virtual appointment <https://app.go.firsttechfed.com/e/er?s=1216207526&lid=8209&elqTrackId=8b291ef30054478bac7af79a0be4cf66m&elq=b10adabd5ee6445b967b3ea0f4575fe1&elqaid=4260&elqat=1> or give us a call at 855.855.8805 Monday – Friday from 6am – 6pm PT. You can also visit us at our nearby locations at Palo Alto (3000 El Camino Real, Suite 100, Palo Alto, CA 94306) or Cupertino (19960 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014).

Thank you for your membership and we look forward to continuing to serve you at our San Jose location. 

Sincerely,

Mildrid Garingarao
San Jose Experience Center Manager
First Tech Federal Credit Union

ref# 2617802_4260

subject: Re: Ramah touch-base
body_text:
Hi Aviva!

Here is the link to his service. It gives a good sense of how far he has
come this year:
https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1MrnV8WJOlm-IJ2VfCasuAkdTtN6r034I?usp=sharing

He went on a three day camping trip with his school in early March and they
said he did great.

Right now, he is away for a week on a school trip to Chicago and Detroit.
He comes home tonight. I will touch base with his advisor (the trip lead)
next week to hear how he did. We received no phone calls or emails or
communication about any issues while he was away. I am eager to hear how he
did in detail as he likely had little sleep and lots of stimulation - which
is the most camp-like conditions his school will observe him in. I can see
from one of the school videos of the trip that he is still Jules though -
when all the other kids are sitting still and listening, he is quiet and
listening while also fidgeting and touching the stuff around him. In one
video, I saw a teacher ask him to stay nearby and he listened quickly, did
not disrupt the group, and stayed where he was supposed to be.

His bar mitzvah party was so fun and nice kids from Ramah came to be with
him and celebrate,so he was very happy. Here are some photos. They include
one of Michaela Miller and Carter Abrams in the photo booth. Max Saraf,
Sarah Goldin, and William Raskin flew in. Eytan Frank came as well. Jules'
theme was car disco. He switched out of his tuxedo jacket into a disco ball
jacket for the dance party!
https://photos.app.goo.gl/vjnAE3NMVe2TDi246


He just had a neuropsych evaluation and we are awaiting the written report.
But in our conference, his specialist reported how much progress he has
made. So, that was really nice.

I think he continues to manage his neurodiversity in terms of focus and has
worked very hard on timeliness (made lots of progress), blending (moved to
a teen-age backpack from wheeling one, wearing jeans - things that were
impossible a year ago), and self-regulation (impulsivity in blurting out or
talking too much) this year. An example of his self-management recently
happened when a kid who mocks him and bullies him regularly was in the
front of the lunch line. Jules can be impulsive and rush to be first in
line, but he spotted the other boy. He waited and allowed many other kids
to go before him until the older boy was gone. He managed his need to be
first and kept himself out of a situation that could have caused conflict
or escalated.

His psychologist Dr. Gooding has had a year of  getting to know him in more
depth and had some breakthroughs as this was the first year he could see
her each week in person. Would you like to speak with her?

He was re-elected to 7th grade rep by his peers for a second term this year
and had a lot of leadership growth - really shining and keeping other kids
organized and getting things done.

Anyway, I'd like to hear the details of how he did on the trip before we
chat, so I can give you the most up-to-date info. I am happy to connect you
with his advisor or his Spanish teacher - both of whom ran the trip.

Could you please let me know if there will be specialist activities this
summer with folks leading activities who are not counselors? Like how
Mindy used to teach kindness is cool club, or how there used to be
woodshop, etc?

Thanks,
Joy



On Fri, Apr 8, 2022 at 10:16 AM Aviva LevineJacobs <Aviva@ramah.org> wrote:

> Dear Joy and Scott,
>
> I hope that you and your family are well. I’m not quite sure how it is
> that we have arrived at spring, but here we are.
>
> If I heard correctly there was recently a simcha in your family for Jules,
> Mazal Tov, how exciting.
>
>
>
> I’d like to find a time to connect and hear how the year the is going for
> Jules and to share with you some of our plans for the summer. We have added
> some additional staff to our summer team to be able to offer support where
> needed. It would be great to hear how things are going in school and to
> develop a plan together pre-summer that we can involve Jules in along the
> way.
>
> I know that Passover is approaching soon. Here
> <https://calendly.com/aviva-2/parent-phone-conversation> are times that
> I’m available or let me know some pockets next week that work well or
> following Passover.
>
>
>
> Shabbat Shalom,
>
> Aviva
>
>
>
> *Aviva Levine Jacobs, PsyD*
>
> She/Her/Hers - *את/שֶלָךְ/היא/שלה*
>
> *Director of Camper Care and Staff Wellness*
>
> 805-857-1354
>
> [image: signature_296898416]
>
>
>

subject: Things Worth Remembering: Finding Meaning in the Madness of War
body_text:
View this post on the web at https://www.thefp.com/p/things-worth-remembering-finding-meaning-in-the-madness-of-war

Welcome to Things Worth Remembering, in which writers share a poem or a paragraph that all of us should commit to heart. This week, former Marine Corps officer Phil Klay marks Veterans Day by reflecting on William Butler Yeats’s “An Irish Airman Foresees His Death”—and what it taught him about the strange horror and beauty of war.
It wasn’t all the yelling and running and push-ups and crawling through mud that I found difficult during my Marine Corps training; it was the boredom.
Fire watch—standing guard while others sleep—is boring. Waiting around at the rifle range is boring. Humping is boring, if only because “humping” does not mean what you think it does (it means a forced march, usually while carrying heavy gear).
Marines dealt with the boredom in different ways. My method was to memorize poetry.
Before going out in the field, I’d print out poems, cut them up into index-card-sized pieces, laminate them, and put them in my breast pocket. Later, I’d be lying behind my rifle, freezing, monitoring my assigned area of the battlefield, but all the while with a little card of poetry in my hand. Every once in a while I’d look at the card and read a line, recite it a few times in my head, and then move on to the next.
I started small and easy with poems by Thomas Hardy [ https://substack.com/redirect/557fc84c-d4c3-4e95-b14f-5b1dc37f4d98?j=eyJ1IjoiMXpiYW9pIn0.x_isyNDJZBwm1T2P5h--XwU8TVqNOVesUrZse-mL6YE ] and Wilfred Owen [ https://substack.com/redirect/ed8234a7-1646-4ae8-9081-8e5cfaa53f99?j=eyJ1IjoiMXpiYW9pIn0.x_isyNDJZBwm1T2P5h--XwU8TVqNOVesUrZse-mL6YE ]. But they lodged in my head too quickly. Steadily, the poems got longer, with less regular rhyme and meter. Once, as we waited for a helicopter to pick up our squad, one of the trainers—a captain who’d served in combat in Iraq—saw me with my little cards and asked me what I was doing. I told him I was memorizing “The Waste Land [ https://substack.com/redirect/5e532cb0-a8a3-489c-8f36-250ca263df74?j=eyJ1IjoiMXpiYW9pIn0.x_isyNDJZBwm1T2P5h--XwU8TVqNOVesUrZse-mL6YE ],” T.S. Eliot’s 434-line modernist poem. He had not expected this answer. And he didn’t like it.
“You like memorizing stuff?” he barked at me. “What are the elements of a nine-line? Have you memorized that?”
A nine-line is how you request a medical evacuation on the battlefield. It has nine elements. I knew zero.
He growled, “When one of your Marines is dying, what are you gonna do, recite poetry at him?”
Well . . . no, probably not. At least, not T.S. Eliot [ https://substack.com/redirect/c92a8094-913f-4bac-a9bc-f3dd2bb9eb9e?j=eyJ1IjoiMXpiYW9pIn0.x_isyNDJZBwm1T2P5h--XwU8TVqNOVesUrZse-mL6YE ]. But rather than argue, I started memorizing the elements of a nine-line—which, since I ended up as a media officer, one of the safer jobs in the military, I luckily never had to use.
Memorizing poetry, on the other hand, has proved to be one of the best things I’ve done for myself. There’s nothing like learning the rhythms of a poem by heart, especially one with such incredible and varied language. And, by spending serious time with some of the richest ideas ever put to page about war, I prepared myself for the moral, spiritual, and political questions all citizens should ask themselves about the killing done in their name.
One of the first poems I tackled as a young lieutenant was William Butler Yeats [ https://substack.com/redirect/b4a6ad4c-948a-47e9-a668-753712635caa?j=eyJ1IjoiMXpiYW9pIn0.x_isyNDJZBwm1T2P5h--XwU8TVqNOVesUrZse-mL6YE ]’s “An Irish Airman Foresees His Death [ https://substack.com/redirect/18324245-f9d6-46b3-87ec-06320a03a459?j=eyJ1IjoiMXpiYW9pIn0.x_isyNDJZBwm1T2P5h--XwU8TVqNOVesUrZse-mL6YE ],” published in 1919 [ https://substack.com/redirect/2dbdc437-e6de-409c-9d81-ca843f095cb4?j=eyJ1IjoiMXpiYW9pIn0.x_isyNDJZBwm1T2P5h--XwU8TVqNOVesUrZse-mL6YE ].
It’s a perfect little poem, and incredibly easy to memorize, with a singsongy abab rhyme scheme and lines in iambic tetrameter [ https://substack.com/redirect/1420779f-0467-4268-96b5-fd8ec3f0eb2b?j=eyJ1IjoiMXpiYW9pIn0.x_isyNDJZBwm1T2P5h--XwU8TVqNOVesUrZse-mL6YE ] which, as is often the case with Yeats, somehow end up feeling like natural speech despite the rigorous perfection of the form.
Yeats also regularly makes parallelisms, such that one line will answer or complete another. At the time, I thought it was a great poem for a young man heading off to war. One of the other lieutenants, Irish by birth and heading to be a pilot, thought so too, and he borrowed my copy so he could memorize it as well. Here is the text:
I know that I shall meet my fate
Somewhere among the clouds above;
Those that I fight I do not hate,
Those that I guard I do not love;
My country is Kiltartan Cross,
My countrymen Kiltartan’s poor,
No likely end could bring them loss
Or leave them happier than before.
Nor law, nor duty bade me fight,
Nor public men, nor cheering crowds,
A lonely impulse of delight
Drove to this tumult in the clouds;
I balanced all, brought all to mind,
The years to come seemed waste of breath,
A waste of breath the years behind
In balance with this life, this death.
Accustomed as we are to the anti-war poets of World War I, it can be jarring to read such a dark but stunningly beautiful poem. The narrator isn’t bloodthirsty or sociopathic, but there is something in warfare he can’t shake: something valuable, something teased at with the maddeningly vague line about the “lonely impulse of delight” driving men to war—an impulse distinct from patriotism.
What is this lonely impulse, if not “law” or “duty”?
The fact is, some people like the work of war. Every year, tens of thousands of young people join the Army or Marine Corps. They want the camaraderie of war, the adventure, the intellectual challenge but also the sheer beauty of it—of night fires and powerful weaponry and superbly trained groups of men and women operating with skill and precision. Beyond that, though, is the encounter with the utter limits of human experience, something the World War I veteran Pierre Teilhard de Chardin [ https://substack.com/redirect/5e6fca8e-2d7b-443f-ad91-af2a04c56430?j=eyJ1IjoiMXpiYW9pIn0.x_isyNDJZBwm1T2P5h--XwU8TVqNOVesUrZse-mL6YE ], in a 1917 essay [ https://substack.com/redirect/0396f0d1-1b4f-43df-acb2-4e24626bb9fe?j=eyJ1IjoiMXpiYW9pIn0.x_isyNDJZBwm1T2P5h--XwU8TVqNOVesUrZse-mL6YE ] on his strange longing to return to the front, described as a “unique atmosphere, penetrating and dense, in which this entire richness of violence and majesty bathes.”
And then there’s the poem’s equanimity about death. As a young man in military training, that attitude seemed admirable to me. It’s also one often expressed by soldiers and Marines.
There’s an illustrative story told by [ https://substack.com/redirect/9aeebbb1-fc9a-418b-aa21-9fced139a48e?j=eyJ1IjoiMXpiYW9pIn0.x_isyNDJZBwm1T2P5h--XwU8TVqNOVesUrZse-mL6YE ] the writer Bing West, who embedded with a Marine infantry battalion during an especially violent deployment in Afghanistan from 2010 to 2011. After the unit had taken multiple casualties, a Marine infantryman posted a set of “Rules of War” on a plywood sign in his unit’s living quarters. Rule No. 1 was, “Young warriors die.” Rule No. 2? “You cannot change Rule No. 1.”
The final rule on the list echoed the first two: “Going out in a hail of gunfire . . . pop dem nugs until thy body runs dry of blood . . . AND LOOK HELLASICK.” Perhaps not as formally rigorous as Yeats, but still compelling.
Back then, as a young Marine in training at Quantico, I wanted to take this stuff at face value and believe that death truly could be met with such calm, or such swagger. It made the ongoing sacrifice of life seem more bearable. I had a vague sense that, perhaps, people I knew would die or suffer grievous injuries. But it hadn’t happened yet. It was still abstract, and it felt like part of the sacrifice necessary for a worthy cause: fighting an insurgency in a faraway country descending into a spiral of death and chaos.
Every year, tens of thousands of young people join the Army or the Marine Corps. They want the camaraderie of war, the adventure, the intellectual challenge but also the sheer beauty of it.
Now, slightly older and looking back, I see the sentiments of the poem differently. It’s not mere bluster—or not entirely. Rather, it’s the speaker, amid the vast, impersonal, and terrifying forces of the battlefield, privately finding a way to confront the unimaginable possibility of his own death.
That’s precisely my reason for this somewhat unconventional pick on Veterans Day. Today isn’t a day for the hallowed dead, but for those who chose to serve. They’re not victims; they might even love their work. And though the crowds and public men talking about democracy [ https://substack.com/redirect/85394edb-d983-497d-8958-04fb859df973?j=eyJ1IjoiMXpiYW9pIn0.x_isyNDJZBwm1T2P5h--XwU8TVqNOVesUrZse-mL6YE ] or the threat of weapons of mass destruction [ https://substack.com/redirect/9c4ee92e-27f6-420b-83e2-5506b4c81e2a?j=eyJ1IjoiMXpiYW9pIn0.x_isyNDJZBwm1T2P5h--XwU8TVqNOVesUrZse-mL6YE ] may have bade them fight, for so many of my generation of Marines and soldiers, those justifications began to seem hollow, false, or simply irrelevant to the deadly work at hand.
Young warriors die. You cannot change that fact, nor can your mind quite grapple with its reality. And so these warriors—many of whom feel irresistibly drawn to the work of war—speak of death in a way that at times that seems distant, or callous, or strange.
Such equanimity is impossible for me now. As I grow older, as I’ve started a family, I think of those who died not only as warriors but as children of grieving parents, partners of grieving spouses, parents of grieving children. I’m not blind to war’s excitement, its beauty, and its occasional necessity, but what I feel most keenly is its tragedy.
So please, memorize Yeats’s poem, and appreciate how strangely beautiful, calm, and terrible it is. And then imagine the speaker surviving his war, and how his reflections might change with age and time.
Things Worth Remembering will be back in your inbox next Sunday. In case you missed it, last week Liel Leibovitz made his case [ https://substack.com/redirect/0fb55014-f8ec-4a5e-a32e-902cdf60d80f?j=eyJ1IjoiMXpiYW9pIn0.x_isyNDJZBwm1T2P5h--XwU8TVqNOVesUrZse-mL6YE ] for why the classic cartoon “Scooby-Doo” offers one of the most enduring windows onto the American soul.

subject: Fwd: We'd love your help with our Hillbrook High School feasibility study
body_text:
you doing this?

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Shannon Hunt-Scott <shannon.hunt@gmail.com>
Date: Mon, Oct 25, 2021 at 10:10 AM
Subject: We'd love your help with our Hillbrook High School feasibility
study
To: Joy Silver <joysilver@gmail.com>, <scott.m.silver@alum.dartmouth.org>


Dear Joy and Scott,

We are sending a reminder in the hope that you will schedule a 45-minute
interview with CCS Fundraising which is conducting the feasibility study to
help Hillbrook evaluate the possibility of building a high school. Your
input is needed to help the Board make this important decision which will
impact the future of Hillbrook School. We want voices from all aspects of
our community and we believe yours is an important one of those voices.

Please click here
<https://calendly.com/hillbrookschool/hillbrook-school-planning-study?month=2021-11>
to
schedule a virtual meeting. If you don't find a time that works for you,
please email Cathy Carlson at ccarlson@hillbrook.org, as we will gladly
accommodate your calendar. If you are hesitating for any reason, please
don't hesitate to reach out to me. I'm more than happy to share my thoughts
on the project and answer any questions you may have.

With gratitude,
Shannon Hunt-Scott
Chair, Hillbrook Secondary School Exploratory Committee


—
*Shannon Hunt-Scott*
President & Co-Founder

www.thescottfoundation.org
linkedin.com/in/shannonhuntscott  <http://linkedin.com/in/shannonhuntscott>

<http://www.TheScottFoundation.org>


---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Mark Silver <msilver@hillbrook.org>
Date: Tue, Sep 7, 2021 at 8:08 AM
Subject: An Invitation from Head of School Mark Silver
To: Kevin and Shannon <shannon.hunt@gmail.com>


View this email in your browser
<https://mailchi.mp/hillbrook/an-invitation-from-head-of-school-mark-silver?e=ec7058bd85>
<https://hillbrook.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fcdbb4398f7f92eadbef6b0a0&id=340db1230e&e=ec7058bd85>

Dear Kevin and Shannon,

We are at an exciting and critical moment in the school’s history. We have
accomplished extraordinary things since the onset of Vision 2020, which
have highlighted our ability to be innovative, responsive, and flexible,
and to successfully deliver on our vision and mission amidst even the most
challenging moments. For years, members of our community have wondered,
“What would it take to create a Hillbrook Secondary School?” We believe the
time is right to revisit this question.

Working in partnership with the Board of Trustees, our leadership team is
actively pursuing the possibility of launching a secondary school as early
as Fall 2023. A key part of this process is a Feasibility and Planning
Study, which will help us determine the level of community excitement and
engagement in this project. Assisting us with this study is CCS Fundraising
<https://hillbrook.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fcdbb4398f7f92eadbef6b0a0&id=6f03b0f294&e=ec7058bd85>,
a consulting firm with almost 75 years of experience in assisting
independent schools and nonprofit organizations, including many in the Bay
Area and Silicon Valley.

Click here to view our background statement
<https://hillbrook.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fcdbb4398f7f92eadbef6b0a0&id=cfe4a95362&e=ec7058bd85>
that
provides an overview of our plans. I would like to invite you to
participate in a one-hour, private interview with a representative from CCS
to offer insights, advice, and counsel on these plans. The conversation
will be private and will be invaluable in helping shape the future of our
school. We look forward to your candid feedback. There is no need to
prepare for this meeting, other than reviewing the background statement.
CCS is happy to meet via Zoom or in person; for a Zoom meeting, please use this
Calendly link
<https://hillbrook.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fcdbb4398f7f92eadbef6b0a0&id=69ea4e2bba&e=ec7058bd85>
to schedule the appointments. For an in-person meeting or if you have
further questions, simply email Cathy Carlson <ccarlson@hillbrook.org>, our
Director of Advancement.

As a school, we believe that our students deserve a secondary school
experience that builds on the exceptional foundation we provide on our JK-8
campus. We are excited to hear from you and other key members of our
community as we continue to explore the possibility of creating a Hillbrook
secondary school that would be unlike any other offered in Silicon Valley.

With appreciation,



Mark Silver
Head of School
Sign Up for a Zoom Interview
<https://hillbrook.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fcdbb4398f7f92eadbef6b0a0&id=1a8f3fcea7&e=ec7058bd85>
Sign Up for an In-Person Interview <ccarlson@hillbrook.org>
View Our Background Report
<https://hillbrook.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fcdbb4398f7f92eadbef6b0a0&id=a0ca992d1a&e=ec7058bd85>
[image: Twitter]
<https://hillbrook.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fcdbb4398f7f92eadbef6b0a0&id=6f6b932e5f&e=ec7058bd85>
[image: Facebook]
<https://hillbrook.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fcdbb4398f7f92eadbef6b0a0&id=b55bb5264a&e=ec7058bd85>
[image: Instagram]
<https://hillbrook.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fcdbb4398f7f92eadbef6b0a0&id=616e2f51e6&e=ec7058bd85>
[image: Website]
<https://hillbrook.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fcdbb4398f7f92eadbef6b0a0&id=2d570448fd&e=ec7058bd85>
*Copyright © 2021 Hillbrook School, All rights reserved.*
You're receiving this email as a trusted member of the Hillbrook community.
Click below to unsubscribe at any time.

*Our mailing address is:*
Hillbrook School
300 Marchmont Dr
Los Gatos, CA 95032-5659

Add us to your address book
<https://hillbrook.us12.list-manage.com/vcard?u=fcdbb4398f7f92eadbef6b0a0&id=141e7bb4d6>


Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can update your preferences
<https://hillbrook.us12.list-manage.com/profile?u=fcdbb4398f7f92eadbef6b0a0&id=141e7bb4d6&e=ec7058bd85&c=31aeb7282b>
or unsubscribe from this list
<https://hillbrook.us12.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=fcdbb4398f7f92eadbef6b0a0&id=141e7bb4d6&e=ec7058bd85&c=31aeb7282b>.

If you interact with Amazon Web Services programmatically using the SDKs, Command Line Interface (CLI), or APIs, you must provide access keys to verify who you are and whether you have permission to access the resources you're requesting. >>>> ---------- Forwarded message ---------
>>>> From: The Colony Homeowners Association <
>>>> communications@thecolonyhoa.org>
>>>> Date: Wed, Dec 10, 2025 at 9:45 AM
>>>> Subject: Power Outage
>>>> To: <scottmsilver@gmail.com>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Power Outage
>>>>
>>>> The Colony HOA Hotline is distributed to homeowners to broadcast
>>>> important and timely information
>>>>
>>>> Power Outage
>>>>
>>>> The Association is aware of the current power outage affecting many
>>>> residences throughout The Colony. We have been in contact with Rocky
>>>> Mountain Power regarding the situation. RMP has informed us that the outage
>>>> is related to planned maintenance and ongoing work taking place at the base
>>>> of the community, just outside the gatehouse. The HOA was not notified of
>>>> the planned maintenance schedule.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Crews are on site and actively working to resolve the issue. At this
>>>> time, RMP has provided an estimated restoration of power by 4:00 p.m. today.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> We will continue to monitor the situation closely and will share any
>>>> updates as they become available.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Thank you for your patience and understanding.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Warm regards,
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Tim Lewis
>>>>
>>>> The Colony HOA
>>>>
>>>> General Manager
>>>>
>>>> 435.432.4000

subject: Re: Mishloach Manot 2025 : The Great Kol Emeth Bake-off
body_text:
i'll check it out
thanks Scott

On Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 10:54 AM Scott Silver <scottmsilver@gmail.com> wrote:

> It looks like relatively cheaply we can use folks who get data from the
> post office on change of address
>
> https://www.ncoasource.com/pricing.htm
>
> I think you give them a file and they give you the changes for a few
> hundred dollars, if i'm reading correctly.
>
> Scott
>
> On Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 9:15 AM Alan Crystal <alancrystal@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> Great idea Jesse, thanks!
>> I'll follow up
>>
>> On Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 9:14 AM Jessie McBeth <jessie.mcbeth@gmail.com>
>> wrote:
>>
>>> Maybe put an announcements at the top of the mishloach Manot anouchement
>>> in the email “have a change in address? for delivery, please make sure your
>>> current address listed for kol Emeth is correct so your gift can be
>>> delivered
>>> -Click the link to the address or
>>> - Put phone number for office and they can correct.
>>>
>>> Should help with a few.
>>>
>>> Jessie
>>>
>>>
>>>
>>> Sent from Gmail Mobile
>>>
>>>
>>> On Sun, Feb 2, 2025 at 8:15 PM Elaine <elainesigal@gmail.com> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Thanks, Jessie, we will do our best.  I will be sure to have the office
>>>> send another note to congregants.  However, we can only go by the
>>>> information we are given.
>>>>
>>>> I appreciate you taking the time to send me this note. We can’t fix
>>>> what’s broken if we don’t know about it.
>>>>
>>>> Best,
>>>>
>>>> Elaine
>>>> Sent from my iPhone
>>>>
>>>> On Feb 2, 2025, at 7:27 PM, Jessie McBeth <jessie.mcbeth@gmail.com>
>>>> wrote:
>>>>
>>>> ﻿
>>>>
>>>> I delivered last year or maybe two years ago. Out of ten, three of the
>>>> addresses were incorrect and I got physically and verbally chased off
>>>> someone’s property.  Unpleasant and mildly scary.
>>>>
>>>> I was wondering if we can confirm addresses before delivery somehow?
>>>>
>>>> Jessie McBeth
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Sent from Gmail Mobile
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> On Sun, Feb 2, 2025 at 6:06 PM Russell Gottfried <
>>>> russellgottfried@gmail.com> wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> Elaine, thank you so much for your leadership and your work with
>>>>> the staff on behalf of the synagogue.
>>>>>
>>>>> Everyone, please lend a hand and help Elaine make this year's
>>>>> Mishloach Manot the most successful yet. Whether it's bagging hamantaschen,
>>>>> packing bags, or delivering, every bit of help counts.
>>>>>
>>>>> Let's show our support -- and give!
>>>>>
>>>>> ~Russ
>>>>>
>>>>>
>>>>>
>>>>> On Thu, Jan 30, 2025 at 5:50 PM Elaine Sigal <elainesigal@gmail.com>
>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>
>>>>>> Hi!
>>>>>>
>>>>>> I hope that you are well.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> As many of you might remember, I brought this community builder and
>>>>>> fundraiser to Kol Emeth over 15 years ago. I actually "borrowed"  the idea
>>>>>> from my Shul in NJ. In fact, for years, I organized the program.  Since the
>>>>>> office needed to take it over when I retired, Mishloach Manot added to the
>>>>>> staff's workload.  This year, I asked to take it back to us, the volunteers
>>>>>> of KE!  Of course, we will still have help from everyone in the office.
>>>>>> (Thank you!)  Another plus, I am pleased to be working with Nechama Tamler
>>>>>> and Linda Ziff from the L’Olam Vaed (Climate Action) Committee.
>>>>>> They have been incredibly helpful in educating me about plastic!
>>>>>>
>>>>>> I need help bagging the hamantaschen, packing the bags, and
>>>>>> delivering the bags.  Might I count on you to help?  See included links to
>>>>>> sign up.  For those of you who could help bagging the hamantaschen, it will
>>>>>> be a day time activity closer to Purim.  I do not have a date yet (I need
>>>>>> to be told from the company when they will ship), but if you think you have
>>>>>> 2-3 hours one morning to help - involves sitting only- please just let me
>>>>>> know. This is a fun "schmoozing" activity.  Ask a friend to join you.
>>>>>> Bonus - you get to eat the broken ones while bagging.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> This year, we have a theme!  *The Great Kol Emeth Bake-off.*  I
>>>>>> "borrowed" this idea from my youngest son's Shul in NJ.  The 2005 mishloach
>>>>>> manot will have healthy snacks, be mostly gluten free, nut free, kosher and
>>>>>> environmentally friendly, as well as having some items to help you cook. I
>>>>>> hope that you will enjoy the "healthy" snacks.  Sarah Miller and I are
>>>>>> organizing a hamantaschen "share" at the carnival and at the beginning of
>>>>>> the Megillah reading.  You should love it. So bake away, and save some to
>>>>>> bring to KE.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> When you place  your order, please think about sending some to
>>>>>> others: local friends, grandparents, parents, adult children, out of town
>>>>>> friends, as well as college students (who will receive a different bag)
>>>>>> These bags will make people smile!  My goal this year is to set a new
>>>>>> record of the number of bags ordered.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> If you already sent in your forms, thank you!  Now, if you would not
>>>>>> mind, please forward this to your KE friends!!!
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Please feel free to email me or call me if you have any questions, or
>>>>>> just want to say hi.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Best,
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Elaine
>>>>>>
>>>>>> https://kolemeth.shulcloud.com/campaign/2025-mishloach-manot.html
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>> https://kolemeth.shulcloud.com/form/2025-mishloach-manot-delivery-volunteer.html
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>> https://kolemeth.shulcloud.com/form/2025-mishloach-manot-assembly-volunteer-form.html
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>> https://images.shulcloud.com/13785/uploads/Kashrut/Kashrut-Guidelines-2019.pdf
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Elaine Sigal
>>>>>> 650-269-3058
>>>>>>
>>>>>>

subject: Re: Great to connect this morning
body_text:
Thanks.

On Sun, Oct 26, 2025 at 8:24 AM Bob Lasher <Bob.Lasher@dartmouth.edu> wrote:

> Thanks for circling back, Scott.
> I've reached out to the CS folks to explore their availability on Friday
> afternoon between 2:30-5:30 pm.  If we can make the stars align, we will!
> Thanks for your patience. Bob
> ------------------------------
> *From:* Scott Silver <scottmsilver@gmail.com>
> *Sent:* Sunday, October 26, 2025 12:16 AM
> *To:* Joyce L. Hammel <Joyce.L.Hammel@dartmouth.edu>
> *Cc:* Bob Lasher <Bob.Lasher@dartmouth.edu>; joysilver@gmail.com <
> joysilver@gmail.com>
> *Subject:* Re: Great to connect this morning
>
> Hi folks -
>
> Great to see you.
> This illustration sounds fine.
>
> Unfortunately, we don't arrive until just before the evening event as I'm
> working in Boston that day.
>
> Is there a way to weave in any of the meetings during the day Friday or on
> the weekend (a tall ask, I know)? I think we should prioritize the CS folks
> as I'm sure Sian's team is even more scarce :-). Alternatively, we could
> follow up via video in the weeks after.
>
> Please let us know,
>
> Scott
>
> On Fri, Oct 24, 2025 at 4:40 PM Joyce L. Hammel <
> Joyce.L.Hammel@dartmouth.edu> wrote:
>
> Joy and Scott,
>
> Great to be with you both this morning. I’m glad these new ideas are
> intriguing.
>
>
>
> Please let me know what time you’ll arrive in Hanover on Thursday. I’ll
> work up a schedule to include time with Devin and Vasanta in addition to a
> Dali Lab introduction with Lori Loeb. We’ll also aim for time with Sian if
> possible.
>
>
>
> Lastly, I’ve included an illustration of a $1M Dartmouth College Fund
> reunion gift over 5 years with room for sketching out further CS
> commitments over the same period. The class of 1996 is aiming high. Thanks
> for inspiring others. A shaded square of quiet support speaks volumes.
>
>
>
> Have a great weekend all,
>
> Joyce
>
>
>
>
>
> *From:* Bob Lasher <Bob.Lasher@dartmouth.edu>
> *Sent:* Friday, October 24, 2025 11:22 AM
> *To:* scottmsilver@gmail.com; joysilver@gmail.com
> *Cc:* Joyce L. Hammel <Joyce.L.Hammel@dartmouth.edu>
> *Subject:* Great to connect this morning
>
>
>
> Hi Joy and Scott:
>
>
>
> Thanks for the time this morning!   We appreciate the encouragement very
> much.
>
> First, let me say that we look forward to putting the Computer Science
> meetings together for next Thursday.  If there is time and you are game,
> I’d like to add 30 mins with Sian if it can be between 12:30 and 2 pm.
> Joyce and I will work that out.  (I’ve just sent out the PLC schedule so
> you should have that now.)
>
>
>
> Second, thank you for considering our $1 million request for the Dartmouth
> College Fund and Class of 1996 reunion.  I’ve asked Joyce to send you back
> a pledge worksheet so you can see what that pledge would look like over 5
> years.
>
>
>
> Finally, here’s a description of the Wild Card idea.
>
>
>
> DALI Lab <https://dali.dartmouth.edu/> gives computer science students
> the chance to work in interdisciplinary teams to develop cutting-edge
> digital solutions for real clients — from startups to non-profits to
> academic researchers. Students not only gain hands-on technical and design
> experience but also sharpen leadership, collaboration, and communication
> skills. The impact is two-fold: students are transformed into confident,
> capable problem-solvers, and clients receive high-quality, innovative
> digital products.
>
>
>
> Since its founding in 2013, DALI has offered hundreds of Dartmouth
> students the chance to contribute to real-world technology projects with
> tangible impact. Students serve as designers, developers, project managers,
> and data scientists, all while receiving mentorship from other students,
> experienced staff, and industry professionals. They also find practical
> uses for emerging technologies, such as developing novel translational
> applications for AI. DALI Lab is a selling point for prospective students
> who see it as a launching pad to train for their dream jobs.
>
>
>
> Here’s the breakdown by gender of the participants and the reason why I
> think its worth a brief exploration:
>
>
>
> *Total Students fall 2025:* 93
>
> *Gender Distribution:*
>
>    - *She/Her:* 53 (57.0%)
>    - *He/Him:* 39 (41.9%)
>    - *Other:* 1 (1.1%)
>
> *Class Year Breakdown:*
>
>    - *Class of '26:* 29 students (31.2%)
>    - *Class of '27:* 31 students (33.3%)
>    - *Class of '28:* 26 students (28.0%)
>    - *Earlier classes ('23-'25):* 4 students (4.3%)
>    - *Other:* 3 students (3.2%)
>
> Lorie Loeb <https://faculty-directory.dartmouth.edu/lorie-loeb> leads
> this entity and is a Computer Science faculty member. Tim Tregubov
> <https://faculty-directory.dartmouth.edu/tim-tregubov> is a director and
> is a lecturer in Computer Science.
>
>
>
>

subject: The Voice of Friendship Circle/ Organization Update - May 2023
body_text:
Highlighting the impact Friendship Circle has on families and volunteers

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/bayareafc/the-voice-of-friendship-circle-organization-update-june-13857354?e=ce8378360a)


** The Voice of Friendship Circle/ Organization Update - May 2023
------------------------------------------------------------

Dear Scott,

It has been an incredible year!  Thank you to all our families and volunteers who make Friendship Circle an inclusive and caring community.  We are sad to say good-bye to some of our teen volunteers who are graduating high school this summer but we are so grateful for your support and friendship these past few years.

We have some exciting new events coming up including our annual WalkWithFC, Giants Game with FC and our Annual Picnic. Everyone is welcome to join in these special events, even if you aren't a Friendship Circle family.  Please come and bring your friends!

Thanks!
Ezzy & Nechama Schusterman
Executive Director

WalkWithFC 2023 - Save the Date 9/10/23

Save the date for WalkWithFC 2023!  We will be holding our yearly community and fundraiser event on Sunday, September 10th from 10:30 to 1:00 at AchieveKids in Palo Alto. This year the theme is Bubblemania! We hope you can join us for a bubble-themed walk around Mitchell Park with an afterparty at AchieveKids that includes a free lunch, bubble fun activities, arts and crafts, games, jumpy houses, a bubble show and a HUGE FOAM PIT!  You don’t want to miss the fun and it’s an amazing opportunity to support the Bay Area Friendship Circle!  More information and registration information here: www.WalkWithFC.com  Help us reach our goal of $150,000!

Program Recap: Palo Alto Junior Museum and Zoo

In April, we were fortunate enough to partner with the Palo Alto Junior Museum and Zoo who opened their doors after business hours so the Friendship Circle could have exclusive access to their facility.  Over 40 of our families came out to explore this amazing and interactive museum, outdoor play structure and meet some cool animals like flamingos and raccoons and we even got to pet a snake!  We encourage you to check out the Palo Alto Junior Museum and Zoo and their inclusive museum.  A special thank you to Lauren Angelo, a parent of a volunteer who is on the Board of Directors and the staff who facilitated our visit!

Meet the newest member of the Friendship Circle family!

On March 14th, our amazing Program Director, Lauren Levinson, gave birth to a beautiful daughter, Ayla!  We couldn’t be happier and more excited for Lauren and her husband Simon!  Lauren and her family have been enjoying a few months together and she will be starting back to run our programs again at the end of June.  If you haven’t seen adorable Ayla yet, please come to our Family Summer Picnic (details below) to meet her in person and also welcome Lauren back to work.

Summer Programming

Just because our Sunday programs are on hold, doesn’t mean we don’t want to see everyone this summer!  Please join us for two fun programs in the month of June!

https://www.bayareafc.org/templates/articlecco_cdo/aid/5519593/jewish/SF-Giants-Game.htm

Giants Game

Come join us to watch the San Francisco Giants vs. the Baltimore Orioles on June 4th at 1:00 pm.  We have a section reserved for our group to cheer on your team!

Family Picnic and Puppet Show at Mitchell Park

Let’s celebrate the start of summer with a fun day in the park.  Bring your picnic lunch and watch a puppet show.  Crafts, games and bubbles will be provided!  And we will have an opportunity to welcome back Lauren to Friendship Circle  (and meet baby Ayla)!!

Feedback?

We love to know what our community thinks. Please take a moment to share your feedback, a testimonial, a note of thanks or anything you’d like to share. We value your input. Please reply to this email or email info@bayareafc.org.
The Year In Pictures

============================================================

** (https://www.bayareafc.org/templates/articlecco_cdo/aid/2147481/jewish/Donate.htm)
Friendship Circle relies on the support of generous donors like you to sustain and grow our programs.  Please consider a gift to the Friendship Circle by going to ** www.BayAreaFC.org/donate (http://www.bayareafc.org/donate)

Copyright © *2019* *Bay Area Friendship Circle*, All rights reserved.

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can ** update your preferences (https://BayAreaFC.us17.list-manage.com/profile?u=79486bfb8979214967cc450c7&id=9a883bde89&e=ce8378360a&c=124ba9f9f7)
or ** unsubscribe from this list (https://BayAreaFC.us17.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=79486bfb8979214967cc450c7&id=9a883bde89&e=ce8378360a&c=124ba9f9f7)
.

>> I don’t have any update today. The investment office is on it and will
>> advise on next steps shortly. I’ve urged them to keep this moving swiftly
>> to meet the year-end deadline.
>>
>>
>>
>> Joyce
>>
>>
>>
>> *From:* Scott Silver <scottmsilver@gmail.com>
>> *Sent:* Friday, December 13, 2024 3:36 PM
>> *To:* Joyce L. Hammel <Joyce.L.Hammel@dartmouth.edu>
>> *Cc:* Joy Silver <joysilver@gmail.com>
>> *Subject:* Re: Great to chat!
>>
>>
>>
>> Ideally Solana but could be Bitcoin.
>>
>> It would be for the balance above (200K)
>>
>>
>>
>> Thanks,
>>
>>
>>
>> Scott
>>
>>
>>
>> On Fri, Dec 13, 2024 at 3:34 PM Joyce L. Hammel <
>> Joyce.L.Hammel@dartmouth.edu> wrote:
>>
>> We are in the process of developing policies and procedures on this front
>> but can accept certain cryptocurrencies as we do this. Please let me know
>> which currency and amount.  I will get a response from our investment
>> office on Monday morning.
>>
>>
>>
>> Joyce
>>
>>
>>
>> Joyce Hammel
>>
>> Dartmouth College
>>
>> West Coast Advancement
>>
>> 802-595-4998
>> ------------------------------
>>
>> *From:* Scott Silver <scottmsilver@gmail.com>
>> *Sent:* Friday, December 13, 2024 3:12:48 PM
>> *To:* Joyce L. Hammel <Joyce.L.Hammel@dartmouth.edu>
>> *Cc:* Joy Silver <joysilver@gmail.com>
>> *Subject:* Re: Great to chat!
>>
>>
>>
>> Joyce,
>>
>>
>>
>> One last question for you. Does Dartmouth accept cryptocurrency gifts?
>>
>>
>>
>> Scott
>>
>>
>>
>> On Mon, Dec 9, 2024 at 11:45 AM Scott Silver <scottmsilver@gmail.com>
>> wrote:
>>
>>
>>
>>
>>
>> On Mon, Dec 9, 2024 at 10:06 AM Joyce L. Hammel <
>> Joyce.L.Hammel@dartmouth.edu> wrote:
>>
>> Hi Scott and Joy,
>>
>> Wonderful news! Thank you for sharing your intentions and interest in
>> more data around WISP outcomes as you consider that allocation for upcoming
>> gifts. Glad to dig in there with you in the months ahead.
>>
>>
>>
>> A few housekeeping questions:
>>
>>    1. Will these gifts be by calendar year end '24 '25 '26 (12/31) or
>>    our fiscal year end (6/30)?
>>
>> Calendar years
>>
>>
>>    1. Is there an amount you may add or carve out to support the
>>    Dartmouth College Fund? While you are parents, directing it to the Parents
>>    and Grandparents Fund is a great way to inspire fellow parents, even
>>    anonymously. That serves as a different door to the same bucket of current
>>    use financial aid; full class credit is given. It is a complement to the
>>    spinoff from endowed funds like your 1024 fund--still crucial until we
>>    fully endow the aid budget (our ultra marathon).
>>
>> I don't know what "full class credit" means.
>>
>> We gave $50K this year to DCF and we will commit to that portion in the
>> subsequent years.
>>
>>
>>
>> Thank you again for support and wise counsel to President Beilock via the
>> PLC.  I am thrilled to be a small part of Dartmouth's next chapter--you and
>> Gigi play a much bigger role!
>>
>>
>>
>> With gratitude,
>>
>> Joyce
>>
>>
>>
>> PS I am out this week but checking email once daily. Keep in touch!
>>
>>
>>
>>
>>
>> Joyce Hammel
>>
>>
>>
>> West Coast Advancement
>>
>> Dartmouth College
>>
>> 802-595-4998
>> ------------------------------
>>
>> *From:* Scott Silver <scottmsilver@gmail.com>
>> *Sent:* Sunday, December 8, 2024 5:39:09 PM
>> *To:* Joyce L. Hammel <Joyce.L.Hammel@dartmouth.edu>
>> *Cc:* Joy Silver <joysilver@gmail.com>
>> *Subject:* Re: Great to chat!
>>
>>
>>
>> Hi there -
>>
>>
>>
>> Joy and I decided to do the following.
>>
>>
>>
>> - We're going to give an additional 200K to the 1024 fund in 2024,
>> bringing our commitment to Dartmouth to $250K this year.
>>
>> - We intend to give the same amount each of the next 2 years (2025 and
>> 2026), also, but we can figure out together how best to allocate it. We are
>> excited about WISP, pointing to future years, and want to learn more about
>> what Dartmouth understands about outcomes for those women who take
>> advantage of it.
>>
>>
>>
>> Thanks,
>>
>>
>>
>> Scott
>>
>>
>>
>> On Fri, Oct 18, 2024 at 12:46 PM Joyce L. Hammel <
>> Joyce.L.Hammel@dartmouth.edu> wrote:
>>
>> Hi Scott,
>>
>> It was great to meet you today and terrific to be with Provost Kotz. It
>> is always interesting to hear Dave’s administrative, faculty, and parent
>> perspectives. A man of many hats and much wisdom. Jinny shared that you and
>> Joy are finalizing your giving plans as year end approaches.  Your two
>> funds are making wonderful impact. If simply adding to these feels
>> right—that is welcome. Other options we discussed are below.
>>
>>
>>
>> To address the underrepresentation of women in STEM without risking
>> future legal constraint, President Beilock has focused on building programs
>> that address diversity in STEM more broadly, but with, as Dave noted, the
>> intent of developing women in CS/Engineering/Life Sciences. Many of these
>> programs are part of the constellation of initiatives within Dartmouth
>> NEXT. The overview of all opportunities is attached for scope, but please
>> note:
>>
>> -BE Scholarship
>>
>> -Emerging Engineers
>>
>> -Learning Fellows
>>
>> -Women in Science Project  (this is ‘unstuck’ from legal, and we can
>> accept gifts. Preference for CS may be too specific)
>>
>>
>>
>> Separately, I appreciate your interest in the Jewish student experience
>> at Dartmouth. It has been an unsettling year for Jewish diaspora to say the
>> least and I hope your family has found comfort in community over the holy
>> days. I’m glad to hear Gigi feels less tension on our campus. Below are
>> three avenues for supporting the Jewish community at Dartmouth.
>>
>>
>>
>> -Curricular or Faculty support for the Jewish Studies Department (details
>> attached)
>>
>> -Dialogues Project which supports academic forums, faculty skills
>> building, and collaborative partnerships. This is not specific to Jewish
>> life but does aim to build a culture of empathy across differences.
>>
>> -Programmatic support for religious communities at the Tucker Center.
>> Judaism may be specified.
>>
>>
>>
>> This is a lot. Please let me know what resonates most and we can refine
>> from there. I am available by phone or Zoom next week, and back in the Bay
>> Area the 27th-29th if an in-person discussion is helpful. Email is
>> always efficient and easy!
>>
>>
>>
>> *Joyce*
>>
>> _____________________________
>>
>> Joyce Hammel
>>
>> Executive Director
>>
>> Western Region Advancement
>>
>> Dartmouth College
>>
>> (m) 802-595-4998
>>
>>
>>
>>
>>
>>

Subject: RE: 316 Costello Project
Rebecca,

We have completed reviewing the elevations from the landscape plans. By way of this email, we would like to issue a headsup on the items that we will be recommending as shown on the attached plans:
-installation of a new sump pump and stormdrain lines to divert the backyard stormwater towards the existing metered release outlet
-installation of new area drains to collect stormwater from the pool deck
Let us know if you have any questions, comments and concerns. Our final step will be updating the hydrology model which we plan to have completed by the end of this week.

Best,
