>
>
> Want to coordinate a ski weekend in winter / early spring with the
> kids?     (I could coordinate with the below invitation if we do it
> soon).
>
>
>
> ---------------------------------------------------------------
>
> Alison Marsden, PhD (she / her)
>
> Douglass M. and Nola Leishman Professor of Cardiovascular Disease
> Professor of Pediatrics and Bioengineering, and by courtesy of Mechanical
> Engineering
> Institute for Computational and Mathematical Engineering
> Stanford University
>
> https://cbcl.stanford.edu/
>
> www.simvascular.org
>
>
>
>
>
> *From: *Jeff Weiss <Jeff.Weiss@utah.edu>
> *Date: *Monday, August 8, 2022 at 3:17 PM
> *To: *Alison Lesley Marsden <amarsden@stanford.edu>
> *Subject: *U. Utah BME seminar invitation (virtual)
>
> Dear Alison,
>
>
>
> I hope all is well with you. I’m writing to ask if you would be willing to
> give a seminar as part of our BME department’s “BME Elevated Seminar
> Series”.
>
>
>
> You could request dates for any Friday during Fall and Spring Semester,
> and our department staff would coordinate with you to arrange a date,
> acquire your talk and speaker information, etc.  Our academic calendar is
> as follows:
>
>
>
> Fall semester, August 22 – December 8
>
> Spring semester, January 9 – April 25
>
>
>
> There is also the option for you to visit in person and give the seminar,
> although it seems that most faculty prefer the Zoom option these days.
> Just want to mention it in case you would like to visit our program and the
> state of Utah J
>
>
>
> Best regards,
>
>
>
> Jeff
>
>
>
>
>
> Jeffrey A. Weiss
>
> Professor, Department of Biomedical Engineering
>
> University of Utah
>
> http://mrl.sci.utah.edu
>
>
>
>

subject: Re: Draft gift trusts
body_text:
Tim -

Can you confirm that privileged will be attached to this email? If so I can
send you an overview of our assets to help with the discussion.

Scott

On Mon, Nov 11, 2024 at 7:34 AM Brugh, Timothy C. <
timothy.brugh@pillsburylaw.com> wrote:

> Hi Scott,
>
>
>
> I’d be glad to discuss.  I’ve copied Melissa to propose some times for a
> zoom call.
>
>
>
> Regards,
>
> Tim
>
>
>
> *From:* Scott Silver <scottmsilver@gmail.com>
> *Sent:* Monday, November 11, 2024 5:30 AM
> *To:* Brugh, Timothy C. <timothy.brugh@pillsburylaw.com>
> *Cc:* Joy Silver <joysilver@gmail.com>
> *Subject:* Re: Draft gift trusts
>
>
>
> Hi Tim -
>
>
>
> Hope you are well.
>
> I'd like to pick up this activity again. Our estate is now on track to
> exceed the lifetime limit -- I suspect regardless of actions by the
> incoming administration -- and so we'd like to make some plans to address
> this.
>
> Can we please set up some time to re-start this effort?
>
>
>
> Scott
>
>
>
> On Tue, Nov 2, 2021 at 3:59 PM Scott Silver <scottmsilver@gmail.com>
> wrote:
>
> Thanks.
>
>
>
> Scott
>
>
>
> On Tue, Nov 2, 2021 at 3:58 PM Brugh, Timothy C. <
> timothy.brugh@pillsburylaw.com> wrote:
>
> Generally $7k-$15k annual range depending upon trustee.  You may check
> with the firm you use for investment services.  Most firms have a DE
> trustee service.
>
>
>
> *From:* Scott Silver <scottmsilver@gmail.com>
> *Sent:* Tuesday, November 2, 2021 3:50 PM
> *To:* Brugh, Timothy C. <timothy.brugh@pillsburylaw.com>
> *Cc:* Joy Silver <joysilver@gmail.com>; Dunn, James <
> james.dunn@pillsburylaw.com>; Fonseca, Melissa <
> melissa.fonseca@pillsburylaw.com>
> *Subject:* Re: Draft gift trusts
>
>
>
> Thanks, any idea how much those fees are?
>
>
>
> Scott
>
>
>
> On Tue, Nov 2, 2021 at 3:46 PM Brugh, Timothy C. <
> timothy.brugh@pillsburylaw.com> wrote:
>
> Yes.  This would be a DE administrative trustee.  Kartik and non-grantor
> spouse could have discretion over trust distributions and you could have
> power to direct the trust investments.
>
>
>
> *From:* Scott Silver <scottmsilver@gmail.com>
> *Sent:* Tuesday, November 2, 2021 3:28 PM
> *To:* Brugh, Timothy C. <timothy.brugh@pillsburylaw.com>
> *Cc:* Joy Silver <joysilver@gmail.com>; Dunn, James <
> james.dunn@pillsburylaw.com>; Fonseca, Melissa <
> melissa.fonseca@pillsburylaw.com>
> *Subject:* Re: Draft gift trusts
>
>
>
> So this is a service provided by a firm for a fee vs Kartik serving as
> trustee?
>
>
>
> Scott
>
>
>
> On Tue, Nov 2, 2021 at 10:53 AM Brugh, Timothy C. <
> timothy.brugh@pillsburylaw.com> wrote:
>
> You will need a DE trustee and there is generally a fee associated with
> appointing an institutional trustee.
>
>
>
> *From:* Scott Silver <scottmsilver@gmail.com>
> *Sent:* Tuesday, November 2, 2021 9:48 AM
> *To:* Brugh, Timothy C. <timothy.brugh@pillsburylaw.com>
> *Cc:* Joy Silver <joysilver@gmail.com>; Dunn, James <
> james.dunn@pillsburylaw.com>; Fonseca, Melissa <
> melissa.fonseca@pillsburylaw.com>
> *Subject:* Re: Draft gift trusts
>
>
>
> I think you answered everything by email except for the DE v CA trust.
>
> Are there any disadvantages of the trust in DE?
>
>
>
> Thanks,
>
>
>
> Scott
>
>
>
> On Mon, Nov 1, 2021 at 4:27 PM Brugh, Timothy C. <
> timothy.brugh@pillsburylaw.com> wrote:
>
> Hi Scott,
>
>
>
> The latest proposed legislation dropped the limitations on SLATs and the
> year end reduction in gift exemption so there is less urgency for the gift
> planning.  I suggest we complete the gift to one trust this year and the
> other trust after the first of the year.  The first step will be to create
> separate property accounts for each of you and Joy.  If you want to use a
> DE trustee, then we can revise the draft trusts to include the DE
> provisions.
>
>
>
> Below are responses to your questions.  I would be glad to schedule a call
> to discuss details.  I know we both start the day early so I can do a call
> as early as 7:30.
>
>
>
> Regards,
>
> Tim
>
>
>
> *From:* Scott Silver <scottmsilver@gmail.com>
> *Sent:* Monday, November 1, 2021 5:23 AM
> *To:* Brugh, Timothy C. <timothy.brugh@pillsburylaw.com>
> *Cc:* Joy Silver <joysilver@gmail.com>; Dunn, James <
> james.dunn@pillsburylaw.com>; Fonseca, Melissa <
> melissa.fonseca@pillsburylaw.com>
> *Subject:* Re: Draft gift trusts
>
>
>
> Hi Tim -
>
>
>
> 1. I think we are 90% there ready to go. I think, unless your advice is
> different, we'd fund to the current max (11.7M?), in the following way:
>
>
>
> a) a few startup investments we have (of limited value) (say 200k)
>
> b) the balance in our most likely to accrue assets -- liquid equity
> investments.
>
>
>
> [This is fine.  You should establish separate accounts for each of you
> and Joy, and then provide account numbers so we can prepare an agreement
> for you and Joy to transmute account assets from community property to
> separate property.  We would also need the identity of the private
> investments for this agreement]
>
>
>
> Though I'd love to do the ski house, it seems that it is both encumbered
> and thus complex and is unlikely to rise in value like these other things.
>
>
>
> 2. I'd like to hear about DE vs CA w.r.t length of trust.
>
>
>
> [Under the CA rule against perpetuities, a trust must generally terminate
> in 90 years, or 21 years after the death of you last living child.  DE has
> no rule against perpetuities so trusts can continue indefinitely for future
> descendants.]
>
>
>
> 3. I read online at Fidelity's info on SLATs that some may have a
> provision to swap assets into the trust of equal value (but may have
> different cost bases). Is that anticipated by this?
>
>
>
> [The draft trusts we prepared include the power to substitute property.
> The initial proposed tax legislation would have limited this option but
> this limitation was not included in the most recent proposed legislation]
>
>
>
> 4. I need to tell Kartik a little more about his responsibilities in all
> this -- his actual work he'd be taking on in practice. Can you summarize
> that for me?
>
>
>
> [I will prepare a separate email summary]
>
>
>
> If some of this is best by phone, let's set something up this week if
> possible. If not email is fine for me, too.
>
>
>
> Thanks,
>
>
>
> Scott
>
>
>
> On Wed, Oct 27, 2021 at 11:07 AM Brugh, Timothy C. <
> timothy.brugh@pillsburylaw.com> wrote:
>
> Hi Scott,
>
>
>
> The trust is required to terminate under CA law upon later of 21 years
> after death of last beneficiary living at time of trust creation or 90
> years.  The trust can be set up under DE law with no termination (rule
> against perpetuity).  You would need to appoint a DE administrative trustee
> but you could maintain control over investments and Kartik could direct
> distributions. I’d be glad to discuss.
>
>
>
> Regards,
>
> Tim
>
>
>
> *From:* Scott Silver <scottmsilver@gmail.com>
> *Sent:* Wednesday, October 27, 2021 11:00 AM
> *To:* Brugh, Timothy C. <timothy.brugh@pillsburylaw.com>
> *Cc:* Joy Silver <joysilver@gmail.com>; Dunn, James <
> james.dunn@pillsburylaw.com>; Fonseca, Melissa <
> melissa.fonseca@pillsburylaw.com>
> *Subject:* Re: Draft gift trusts
>
>
>
> Hi Tim -
>
>
>
> I am working on thinking through the assets.
>
> When will this trust likely expire based on current law? I have read about
> (and in the trust) dates like 21 years or 90 years.
>
>
>
> Thanks,
>
>
>
> Scott
>
>
>
> On Mon, Aug 16, 2021 at 8:39 AM Brugh, Timothy C. <
> timothy.brugh@pillsburylaw.com> wrote:
>
> Hello Scott and Joy,
>
>
>
> Attached for review are the following documents:
>
>    - Silver Family Irrevocable Trust (Trust formed by Scott for benefit
>    of Joy and descendants)
>    - Memo summarizing terms of Silver Family Irrevocable Trust
>    - Kimberly J. Silver Trust (Trust formed by Joy for descendants; Scott
>    becomes beneficiary after 5 years)
>    - Memo summarizing terms of Kimberly J. Silver Trust
>
>
>
> I would be glad to schedule a call to review the terms of the trusts.
>
>
>
> As next steps, you should identify which assets will be gifted to the
> trusts.  We will need to prepare a marital agreement to transmute community
> property to your respective separate property for purposes of the gifts,
> and prepare deeds to retitle real property according to the marital
> agreement prior to gifts of real property.
>
>
>
> We will need also need to obtain appraisals for real property that will be
> gifted to the trusts.
>
>
>
> I have some suggestions for the gifts that would probably be easier to
> discuss on a call.
>
>
>
> Regards,
>
> Tim
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> *Timothy C. Brugh* | Partner
>
> Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP
>
> 2550 Hanover Street | Palo Alto, CA 94304-1115
>
> t +1.650.233.4092
>
> timothy.brugh@pillsburylaw.com | website bio
> <https://www.pillsburylaw.com/en/lawyers/timothy-brugh.html>
>
> <http://www.pillsburylaw.com>
>
>
>
>
>

subject: Does “bimonthly” mean twice a month or every two months?
body_text:
https://track.wordsmarts.com/?&eih=rgu53zsphz58u1kwls7qkt2ptbrc4vayo14&__stmp=su8pg2&__onlt=t&email=scottmsilver%40gmail.com

Daily edition

https://track.wordsmarts.com/?&eih=rgu53zsphz58u1kwls7qkt2ptbrc4vayo14&__stmp=su8pg2&__onlt=t&email=scottmsilver%40gmail.com

Vocabulary
https://track.wordsmarts.com/?&eih=rgu53zsphz58u1kwls7qkt2ptbrc4vayo14&__stmp=su8pg2&__onlt=t&email=scottmsilver%40gmail.com

Does “bimonthly” mean twice a month or every two months?
https://track.wordsmarts.com/?&eih=rgu53zsphz58u1kwls7qkt2ptbrc4vayo14&__stmp=su8pg2&__onlt=t&email=scottmsilver%40gmail.com

People think that “bimonthly” is a confusing term with multiple meanings. Well, we’re here to tell you that those people … are spot on.

Bennett Kleinman

https://track.wordsmarts.com/?&eih=rgu53zsphz58u1kwls7qkt2ptbrc4vayo14&__stmp=su8pg2&__onlt=t&email=scottmsilver%40gmail.com

S ome people believe that the word “bimonthly” refers to an event that takes place every two months. Others claim it actually describes something that happens twice per month. Well, at the risk of delighting and upsetting both sides of the argument, I’m here to tell you the truth is that “bimonthly” can be used as an adjective, noun, or adverb to imply either twice a month or every two months.

The prefix “bi-” means “two,” “twice,” “double,” etc., and comes from the Latin bi- with a similar definition. It indicates two of something but is vague with regard to frequency. This creates a bit of a headache when affixing it to time-based words such as “monthly” or “weekly.” A “bi-” prefix can imply “two times each” or “every two,” both being technically correct in an etymological sense.

The best way to alleviate this confusion, short of coining a new word, is to use a different prefix that already exists: “semi-.” The “semi-” prefix means “half,” suggesting the topic is divided into two equal parts. “Semimonthly” means an event happens twice per month. If it’s used in the context of an announcement for a lineup of bimonthly and semimonthly events, for example, folks will know which occur every two months and which they should show up for twice a month.

However, for yearlong periods, things are a bit different. English has two distinct terms: “biannual” (“twice per year”) and “biennial” (“every two years”). The similar spellings might trip people up on the difference between the terms, but it’s likely some people may not even realize “biennial” exists as a distinct word. This is another case where you can swap in the “semi-” prefix to help differentiate between the two — “semiannual” is more commonly used in advertisements and announcements.

But to answer our original question, “bimonthly” can be used for either time period, and now you have an etymology lesson in your pocket to counter any naysayers.

subject: Changes to our Customer Relationship Summary and annual disclosure
body_text:
What’s changed in our disclosures
Hi Scott,
As 2021 comes to a close, we’d like to share a few changes to our disclosures with you.
We recently updated our Customer Relationship Summary (Form CRS) to include more information about the brokerage services we offer you.
Here’s what changed:
  • We added information on our new investment recommendations feature that we offer to certain customers at no charge. We also added an explanation of our legal obligations when we make recommendations, which you can learn more about in our Regulation Best Interest disclosure. 
  • We added details to clarify our existing fees and information on expense ratios. These are underlying fees that customers pay that are charged by the firms that manage the exchange-traded funds (ETFs) that we recommend to you. Our fee schedule hasn’t changed and investing with Robinhood is commission-free, as always. 
  • We clarified some of our conflicts of interest, including adding some new details on fractional shares and principal trading.
You can find the full document in our disclosure library, along with other documents that include details on account privacy, financial protection, and more. Our annual disclosure also outlines a lot of important information about our business practices.
We’re excited to bring you new features next year and want to thank you for being a customer—we’re so glad you’re with us!
– The Robinhood Team
Home
Help Center
Contact Us
Privacy Policy
All investments involve risk and loss of principal is possible. No investments are FDIC insured.
Robinhood Financial LLC is a registered broker-dealer (member SIPC). Robinhood Securities, LLC (member SIPC) is a clearing broker-dealer, which services your account by executing, clearing, and settling your securities trades; preparing and distributing your account statements and trade confirmations; and extending credit to margin enabled accounts. Cryptocurrency services are offered through an account with Robinhood Crypto, LLC (NMLS ID 1702840). Robinhood Crypto is licensed to engage in virtual currency business activity by the New York State Department of Financial Services.  All are subsidiaries of Robinhood Markets, Inc. (‘Robinhood’).
© 2021 Robinhood Markets, Inc.

subject: RE: colony UBB Backup Confirmation
body_text:
Good Morning All,

With yesterday’s storm and the potential for another this afternoon, we will have to reschedule our site visit today.  Our plan was to wrap up the VPN access through the Starlink and possibly check routing through the UBB link.

Does next Thursday 2/20 work for everyone at The Colony and UBB for another failover test?

Thanks,

From: Larry Cohen <Larry@lcjc.com>
Sent: Wednesday, February 12, 2025 4:56 PM
To: Martin Dave <dave@utahbroadband.com>
Cc: Mitch <mmitchell@utahbroadband.com>; Jeske David <david.jeske@gmail.com>; Silver Scott <scottmsilver@gmail.com>; Taylor Mertlich <tmertlich@compunet.biz>; Clegg Christian <christian@thecolonyhoa.org>; Fullmer Heather <heather@thecolonyhoa.org>; Nathan Lourido <nlourido@compunet.biz>; Matt Heiden <mheiden@compunet.biz>
Subject: Re: colony UBB Backup Confirmation

*EXTERNAL*
Thanks Dave, curious how it got resolved?

When can we schedule a failover test that can be supported by UBB and compunet? 11am weekdays seems good for us.

For both sides do we think a much quicker, unnoticeable cutover is possible? 180 seems long?

Thanks


Larry Cohen
Cell 908-279-5577


On Feb 12, 2025, at 3:39 PM, Dave Martin <dave@utahbroadband.com<mailto:dave@utahbroadband.com>> wrote:
﻿
The issue with our peers should be resolved. I see your routes via UBB in several looking glasses (with the 5 prepends).

Depending on how you are testing, and how you are connected to lumen, failover may take as long as 180 or slightly more seconds (if using the default BGP hold timers). If lumen (and your router(s)) is able to detect failure faster (for instance, loss of link that is visible on both sides, or use of BFD, or you administratively shut down your peering with them) failover should occur much faster.

On Wed, 12 Feb 2025 at 12:00, Larry Cohen <Larry@lcjc.com<mailto:Larry@lcjc.com>> wrote:
Hi,

I wanted to loop you both in to figure out or plan going forward. During the test and call last week we had some issues and were planning on testing again this week.

Compunet fixed some config issues and UBB was going to find out why lumen and other peers were rejecting our advertisements. Also, whether we needed to add rpi routing security to ARIN.

Can you let us know if you have found out anything and your readiness and availability to do another failover test.

Thx,

Larry
Larry Cohen - 908-279-5577
Larry@lcjc.com<mailto:Larry@lcjc.com>


On Feb 11, 2025, at 5:51 PM, Larry Cohen <Larry@lcjc.com<mailto:Larry@lcjc.com>> wrote:
﻿ What we were thinking of, is 3 subnets operating as you described (Lumen preferred , BGP, quick failover, etc).

And 1 subnet operating in the reverse , primary UBB, backup Lumen. Not sure why each subnet can’t have its own BGP setup as you described?

If you had 8 paths to the different ISPs you could have a primary and and a backup for each of the 4 subnets. If 4 of the isps were Lumen and 4 UBB, would that be the same?

We can discuss on the phone if it’s easier?

Also, do you have any of the UBB engineering contacts we talked to on the Teams call? Having trouble tracking them down..

Thx
Larry Cohen
Cell 908-279-5577


On Feb 11, 2025, at 5:32 PM, Matt Heiden <mheiden@compunet.biz<mailto:mheiden@compunet.biz>> wrote:
﻿
We did work on the internal routing on the LAB and there are no issues with the routing internally on your switching when failing over to UBB. That part has been confirmed.

When we talk about how to split what subnets are using UBB and Lumen, we need to discuss about how routing is setup, UBB as a backup link. Currently when UBB advertises a default route to us, we are modifying the “local preference” to prefer this route as the “less preferred” path outbound. Lumen is the preferred default route being advertised to us if BGP is up and established with them.

On the flip side, when we are advertising your public IP address spaces to UBB, we are “pre-pending” so when UBB receives our advertisements the path back to Colony will look less preferred than Lumen. This will force outbound and inbound traffic to/from the internet to use Lumen circuit over UBB.

When Lumen goes down, the backup route from UBB will kick in and will start sending out to UBB immediately. If the advertisements over UBB/Lumens networks are advertising correctly, (currently not), traffic will return to Colony over UBB.

If you prefer to use both links as “equal paths” we can configure ECMP (equal cost multi-path)  for both links to be in operation at the same time, using both Lumen and UBB for all internet traffic.

Do you know what you want that routing to look like? For example: send half the traffic over Lumen and the other half over UBB based off source subnet?

Let us (Compunet) discuss options once we know exactly how you want the routing to function between both ISPs.

Thank you




[cid:image001.png@01DB7EBF.832B2DE0]<http://www.compunet.biz/>

Matt Heiden / Senior Systems Engineer / (801) 747-5013<tel:+18017475013>
CompuNet, Inc / http://www.compunet.biz<http://www.compunet.biz/>

*EXTERNAL*
Heather we need to get UBB engineering on this thread, they do owe us an understanding of why our advertising was being rejected and whether we need some Arin changes for routing security...

But, also, Compunet was going to go back to the lab to test the changes needed for re- configuration, that you saw during the testing.

Also,
We also would like to revisit having some more sophisticated options related to routing, especially now that you are going back to the lab. Meaning, the ability to have subnets over both paths simultaneously based on priorities in addition to failover should either link go down and visa versa.



________________________________

Taylor and I are planning on going back up on Friday to get the Starlink VPN tuned as that was not allowing VPN through their modem. We need to log into the modem to troubleshoot.

On Feb 6, 2025, at 4:25 PM, Larry Cohen <larry@lcjc.com<mailto:larry@lcjc.com>> wrote:

﻿

Hi all,



Thanks for coming out today and seems like we may be able to try again next week after some config testing in the lab, Lexie and Heather for setting up and supporting the call.



UBB figuring out why we were getting rejected by their peers and Lumen when we tried to propagate routes and route propagation.





As far as routing security which UBB suggested we may need,



David and I discussed that the first thing we need is to make sure, is that  the Starlink is up and operational before we make more config changes. This provides a fast, out of band, access solution for Compunet to get at our configs from the “inside” should we need config changes or reversions and you aren’t onsite.



We also would like to revisit having some more sophisticated options related to routing, especially now that you are going back to the lab. Meaning, the ability to have subnets over both paths simultaneously based on priorities in addition to failover should either link go down and visa versa.



Also, I don’t have UBB’s comtact information, but, I feel their engineering needs to be included in the design, testing and cutover phase. Most of those conversation will be between Compunet and UBB (we can join if helpful). Who will be making sure that happens?



Thanks,



Larry



Here’s what I found on ARIN’s web site.



Note: ARIN still has us as having 3 nsubnets (1 /23 AND 2 /24S). I heard we advertise as 4 /24s and was concerned about this routing security discussed and if it needs to match ARIN’s inventory?



We are eligible for IRR and RPKI and can sign up for RPKI.



There’s. 2 models of RPKI Hosted and Delegated (assume we want hosted?)



Actually in clicking around we are now enrolled in hosted RPKI but I stopped short of creating an ROA.



Just let me know what we need and I can add it.



Thx

Hi Larry,



The plan tomorrow will be as follows:



UBB/Failover testing:

  1.  Disconnect the Lumen connection and ensure traffic failover to UBB. Monitor the UBB connection via Cloudvision and test connectivity from homes. Document time of failover
  2.  Connect Lumen back to Arista Switch and monitor failback to Lumen. Document failback time.



CheckMK has internal and external IPs assigned to the monitoring PC. The public IP address of the monitoring PC is only accessible from your (Colony Homes) Public IP addresses. This is not accessible from anyone on the internet. The Local IP address will only be accessible over VPN.



[cid:image003.png@01DB7EBF.832B2DE0]

[cid:image004.png@01DB7EBF.832B2DE0]

We need an A-record created to point this URL to the public IP address of Starlink. Your VPN certificate is expired so we will send David a new CSR to get that signed so users can access GloblaProtect (VPN) without having to install a local certificate. We will send further instructions for VPN.
  2.  We will check on the performance PC for functionality.



If there are any other items, please let us know. We will see you tomorrow at 11AM.



Thank you

  2.  Make sure Starlink is correctly configed and operational for our out of band administrative needs.

Larry

The ability to have some traffic go one way and the other down the other pipe and failing successfully in both directions does not seem like it shouldn’t work if configured properly in a bgp environment.



Need to talk more on that.

Larry Cohen - 908-279-5577

Larry@lcjc.com<mailto:Larry@lcjc.com>



On Jan 28, 2025, at 2:01 PM, David Jeske <david.jeske@gmail.com<mailto:david.jeske@gmail.com>> wrote:

﻿





On Tue, Jan 28, 2025 at 11:33 AM Taylor Mertlich <tmertlich@compunet.biz<mailto:tmertlich@compunet.biz>> wrote:

After our call on Friday I reviewed the ask to advertise the unused /24 subnet out the UBB connection with some other CompuNet engineers.  While it is possible to only advertise the network over the UBB connection, only return traffic will go through UBB.  Traffic leaving The Colony will still go out via Lumen.  This has to do with the way we are peering The Colony’s network with both Lumen and UBB via BGP.



Is this happening because the network path preference is being handled automatically via routing-metric cost?



If so, why can't we set one block of IPS to have a lower advertised metric on UBB and another to have a lower metric on Lumen?



I'll check with Heather on asking UBB to use their speed test server.






--

 Senior Network Engineer

*Terms & Conditions*

This ticket is non-refundable unless the original ticket was issued at a
fully refundable fare. Some fares may not allow changes. If allowed, any
change to your itinerary may require payment of a change fee and increased
fare. Failure to appear for any flight without notice to Delta will result
in cancellation of your remaining reservation.

Note: When using certain vouchers to purchase tickets, remaining credits
may not be refunded. Additional charges and/or credits may apply.

Fare Details: SJC DL SLC166.51XAVNA0CE DL SJC255.81LA0NA0IE USD422.32END ZP

†All SkyMiles® program rules apply. To review the rules, see Membership

responsibility of the passenger and must be paid at the time the ticket is
booked. Award Travel seats are limited and may not be available on all

restrictions may apply.

*Checked Bag Allowance*

*On Delta® operated flights, you may carry on one bag and a small personal
item at no charge.

Delta One®/First/Business Class weight allowance reverts to 50 lbs for all
checked bags beyond regular free allowance.

At the time of check in with Delta, SkyMiles Medallion members, SkyTeam
Elite & Elite Plus and active US Military personnel are eligible for fee
waivers and other benefits. For more details, visit delta.com/baggage
<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=b4dbddd4fb09b5588d8fb0f604b2d22455968a17054e04acf6457766b2e996680d39ef3c35ffea0b7b551e8add279d30ebdc93077154c137ce7039b2fc167bcae3ec0f85bcf61411>.
Basic Cardmembers with a Gold, Platinum, or Reserve Delta SkyMiles Credit

<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=b4dbddd4fb09b5582a79f112844a9047722b26bf4e223b40b7df417ab8eb1abecc5dc83a2c5e58ae6d38422a148618ead2ba11cd102d90a96f158d0e60eb3cd37e7d6befe7f7d76d>

A standard checked bag with Delta may be up to 50 lbs and 62 linear inches
(per piece). Additional fees apply for oversize, overweight, and/or
additional pieces of checked baggage. Please review Delta's baggage
guidelines for details. Weight and size restrictions may vary when checking
baggage on carriers other than Delta. Contact with the operating carrier
for detailed checked baggage allowances. You must be checked in at the gate
by the applicable check-in deadlines or your reservation may be cancelled.
Please review Delta's check-in requirement guidelines for details. Check-in
requirements vary by airline, so if your ticket includes travel on other
airlines, please check with the operating carrier on your ticket.

to share them.

*ADVICE TO INTERNATIONAL PASSENGERS ON LIMITATIONS OF LIABILITY*

Passengers embarking upon a journey involving an ultimate destination or a
stop in a country other than the country of departure are advised that the
provisions of an international treaty (the Warsaw Convention, the 1999
Montreal Convention, or other treaty), as well as a carrier’s own contract
of carriage or tariff provisions, may be applicable to their entire
journey, including any portion entirely within the countries of departure
and destination. The applicable treaty governs and may limit the liability
of carriers to passengers for death or personal injury, destruction or loss
of, or damage to, baggage, and for delay of passengers and baggage.

Additional protection can usually be obtained by purchasing insurance from
a private company. Such insurance is not affected by any limitation of the
carrier’s liability under an international treaty. For further information
please consult your airline or insurance company representative.

*Conditions of Carriage*

Air transportation on Delta and the Delta Connection® carriers is subject
to Delta's conditions of carriage
<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=b4dbddd4fb09b5587881a35064215d707829d8472bd21ff82f81691f30ae0ce81231b1d95bc16f9e34ebc815045863f911c76bcd1e7965b1613435df041b41d3>.
They include terms governing for example:

• Limits on our liability
<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=b4dbddd4fb09b55876f72dd9bfde42320acef54e2c02489cc615368be8c9c8f9092969f14dd6aac173a859d693a855cc117f0b1bcfea55a0ad180f2b3e98784146e2036b95d8e90d>

• Claim restrictions
<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=b4dbddd4fb09b558bfc03cec70837214ccbb2fa9019fe9328bd834a47d862d913e554d2f21b8cb18d99dbe548cde922441e1e4504d8bbaaf06a1151fd745ad52651666d35305aef8>
including time periods within which you must file a claim or bring action
against us.
• Our right to change terms
<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=b4dbddd4fb09b5589dcc71c35ab0dd2f122a27029cb30cfd5e2df93154547ee712e98701195e5fe474da6fa6b32cd75d7e5c5a0548140a1267087a969b5897b75b9eae14db523059>
of the contract.
• Check-in requirements
<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=b4dbddd4fb09b5582b31d79b358cf221fc12a9496a01e4421f2e0a697023eb70dce072d07e9aace2b37e3efbe629870f53d228468bdcf538c48aa6cc048acdb5e12bc27510732405>
and other rules established when we may refuse carriage
<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=b4dbddd4fb09b558486e3087293a0d5c78799010f2a116586d6b6a93f3a3a19e562a2feba53152b65d4fd8875fcd07c34a707c45b8c377d624b8caec0f0694d96611ac593a6462a8>
.
• Our rights and limits of our liability for delay or failure to perform
service
<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=b4dbddd4fb09b55881dacc8a8fcff37ebde1e772dea8399fd10843368827b7331abf98c260ee28e3e6059abfe951731d699f8404e1aa99d0b39ab11866b2b9219870733faff9fe0f>
including schedule change, substitution of alternative air carriers or
aircraft, and rerouting.
• Our policy on overbooking flights
<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=b4dbddd4fb09b558368b70655a1e79ad6bcc1710c085cf3daf9f7b39a01a6c223267b3f67dfd07fae4335c40a32295878e98ea409a86d332ea2d2983bba7ad6a5ce3c3048605b2c5>,
and your rights if we deny you boarding due to an oversold flight.

These terms are incorporated by reference into our contract with you. You

This looks great

I have a few questions

1. Does the entry area/way have a design where folks could come in dripping wet from the pool and safely/reasonably get to the bathroom.

3. Will the bedroom fit a king?

4. Can we have a wood door with a fair bit of glass it for the entry door?

Scott

subject: E News 8.1.24
body_text: Temple Har Shalom, Your Jewish Home in the Mountains August 1 2024 26 Tamuz 5784 Shabbat at a Glance ﻿Friday, August 2nd at 6:30pm-Shabbat Services Saturday, August 3rd at 9:00am-Torah Study Upcoming Events Friday, August 9th at 5:30pm-Shabbat Nosh Friday, August 16th at 5:00pm-Security Training: Secure Community Network Friday, August 23rd at 5:00pm-Security Training: Secure Community Network Friday, August 23rd at 5:30pm-Family Services (note date change) Sunday, August 25th at 9:00am-Mitzvah Day Friday, September 6th at 6:30pm-HaBonim honoring June & Steve Volk All Community participants in Good Standing should have received their 2024-2025 engagement information via email. We look forward to all of our community inGood Standing joining us for the High Holy Days this year. If you did not receive an email in regard to 2024-25 please contact Deb Sheldon. You can find our President's letter and FAQ's on our community/membership page on our website. ﻿If you have any questions please contact Wayne Crouch or Deb Sheldon. UPDATE AND REMINDER - Sign Up for Settled Rabbi Search Community Conversation Session The Settled Rabbi Search Committee is in the final stages of obtaining feedback from the THS Community. Below is our proposed timeline (subject to minor modifications) for the next six weeks: Sunday, August 18th – 4:00 – 6:00 PM – in person at THS –CHILDCARE WILL BE AVAILABLE BUT YOU MUST LET US KNOW IF YOU WANT IT! – Email Donna Gordon for childcare. To sign up for the Community Conversation,- RSVP HERE Monday, August 19th – THS Community Questionnaire will be emailed. Everyone is strongly encouraged to respond – even if you participated in a Community Conversation, we want to hear from you. Friday, August 30th – This is the deadline for responding to the Questionnaire. Monday, September 9th – The Search Committee will email a detailed report with consolidated data from our Community Conversations and Questionnaire. Tuesday, September 10th – The Search Committee will present the final version of our Rabbi Application to the Board for approval. Thursday, September 15th – The final Rabbi Application (subject to Board approval) will be submitted to the CCAR (Central Conference of American Rabbis) for posting on its website for prospective applicants to view. Please email me with any questions or comments. We want to hear from you! Donna Gordon, Search Committee Chair THS Community Shabbat Nosh Friday, August 9th at 5:30pm We hope to see all of you!!!!! Whether you are new to the community, here seasonally, or spend all your days in Utah, we hope you will join us for our last summer Shabbat nosh. Please help us plan appropriately and click here to let us know you are coming! 2 Opportunities: Safety and Security Friday, August 16th at 5:00 pm  Stop the Bleed Training Dave McKean - Utah Jewish Community Security Advisor (SCN) We invite the THS community to attend the Stop the Bleed Safety course. This training provides members with the skills to save lives by applying emergency first aid. Click Here to Register for 8/16/24 Friday, August 23rd at 5:00 pm Guardian Safety and Security Training Dave McKean- Utah Jewish Community Security Advisor (SCN) ﻿ Guardians take personal responsibility for the safety and security of themselves, families, and community. Learn skillsets that help you identify, protect, and respond to all hazards, enabling you to become an Active Bystander in your community.  Click Here to Register for 8/23/24 Family Shabbat Temple Har Shalom 5:30pm August 23rd (Note date change!) Ages Preschool-3rd grade Join us for our monthly family service at the Temple. We will have a brief song session and service followed by ﻿age-appropriate activities. Pizza provided!   Upcoming family Shabbat dates September 13th & October 18th. Starting in November, Family Shabbat will be the 2nd Friday of each month. Please RSVP to Lynne by Friday morning so we can order appropriately THS Mitzvah Day Sign-up Click each organization to access the sign-up Jewish Family Service Food Pantry Help sort donations at the Alex & Sally Lebwohl Food Pantry at Jewish Family Service in Salt Lake City. Carpooling will be available. Swaner The Swaner Preserve permanently protects 1,200 acres of open space near Kimball Junction in Park City. Help maintain the incredible, natural landscape of this special location - good for young families. Nuzzles & Co Rescue Ranch Adults and children are welcome to volunteer to possibly walk dogs, give dogs baths, cuddle & brush cats, make dog and cat enrichment, help with cleaning and perform other tasks on their beautiful sprawling grounds that may arise. Located approximately 20 minutes from THS. Carpooling will be available. People’s Health Clinic and Park City Senior Center Both of these wonderful organizations need our help. Please join us to assemble Hygiene and Snack Kits for the Senior Center and assemble crucial Dental Hygiene Kits that will be gifted to community members in need at People's Health Clinic.  Gift of Life It takes less than 5 minutes to potentially save a life. Complete a swab kit and this will allow you to be included in the Gift of Life Marrow Registry in order to match you to a patient in need now or in the future. You have the power to save a life! The ideal age is 18-35, but anyone under 60 can register. This can be done the morning of Mitzvah Day but if you are not available then please let us know and we can schedule another time for you to support Gift of Life. Hope Alliance For this mitzvah project, sponsored by Women of Har Shalom, we will label eyeglasses with appropriate prescription, sort eyeglasses and cases as well as verify prescriptions. The Hope Alliance Vision program uses recycled and new eyeglasses to help those in need experience corrected vision. Primary Children’s Hospital Create cozy fleece blankets for young patients at Primary Children’s Hospital. All ages welcome & no sewing skills necessary. Bridge 21 Bridge21 is creating housing options offering support for a healthy and sustainable lifestyle for neurodiverse adults in Park City. They just broke ground on their first facility and would like our help painting a mural to hang in the home. We'll supply the paints, canvas and fun with this project. Peace House We'll be in back-to-school mode packing backpacks with essential school supplies for children at Peace House. This Mitzvah project, sponsored by Women of Har Shalom, will take place at THS.  THS Beautification  Spend time helping to enhance our beautiful THS property. Kimball Arts Center   More specific details to come - activity will include supporting the Kimball Arts Center If you have any questions please contact Suzanne Jacoby. Please join us! FRIDAY, September 6th at 6:30 PM for a Special Shabbat service and ﻿HaBonim Program In Honor of June & Steve Volk ﻿ Our HaBonim recognition honors those who have dedicated themselves to the growth and sustainability of Temple Har Shalom. We invite you to attend a very special Shabbat service and HaBonim program honoring the Volks on Friday, September 6th, at 6:30 PM, at Temple Har Shalom. Services will be followed by a celebratory Oneg where the Volk's extraordinary dedication to Temple Har Shalom will be recognized. Please click here so we know you are coming and can order appropriately If you would like to make a donation in honor of the Volks please click here THS Ladies Book Club Tuesday, August 21st at 7:00 pm Book in discussion Great Circle by Maggie Shipstead ﻿Click Here to RSVP Wish your family and friends a Sweet & Happy New Year! Send the sweetest of all Rosh Hashanah greetings! Order Today - Free shipping through August 8th! Just in time for Rosh Hashanah on October 3rd, we will ship an 8 oz. jar of Kosher honey with a personalized card: Shana Tova - Wishing you a Healthy and Happy New Year. Your name..... Cost is $14.00 per jar Order by August 8th for free shipping within the U.S. Additional fees apply for international orders. ﻿After August 8th add $8.00 per jar for shipping. Order by September 12th for delivery by Rosh Hashanah. Orders will be accepted until Rosh Hashanah (subject to availability after September 12th and late shipping charges applies after August 8th). Ordering tips from Honey form the Heart. Use a laptop or desktop computer only to place your order. Orders cannot be made on a mobile phone. If you are shipping to an apartment, include the apartment number on the street address line. If you have any trouble placing your order contact Betsy Wertheimer, but don't give up! Bfwertheimer@gmail.com Click Here to Place Your Order On October 7, 2023, during the holiday of Shmini Atzeret, Hamas terrorists launched an unprecedented attack on Israel from Gaza, firing thousands of rockets, breaching the country’s borders, with significant numbers killed and hospitalized. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu declared that Israel was at war to defend itself and gave the order for the IDF to commence "Operation Swords of Iron.” In response to this crisis, United Jewish Federation of Utah has created the "Utah Stands with Israel Fund". Through the Jewish Federation of North America's network of partners, 100% of your donation will go to support the victims of the attack and their families. Am Yisrael Chai. The People of Israel Live. Alex Shapiro, Executive Director United Jewish Federation of Utah Donate to the Utah Stands with Israel Fund Life and Legacy A Grinspoon Foundation Philanthropic Initiative Fifty-two Temple Har Shalom families are members of the Temple’s Life and Legacy Society . They have all committed to the sustainability and future growth of Temple Har Shalom by committing to make a legacy gift that reinforces our Jewish values of philanthropy and commitment to a ﻿Jewish life in Park City. Anyone can become a member of the Life and Legacy Society by making a gift at this time or committing to make an after-life gift. There are many ways to do this and every gift is significant. For additional information or to join the Society, please go to https://hgf.org/programs/life-legacy or contact Joy Erickson or Bill Becker. Visit Ridge Club Here Donations Week of July 29 Harriet Spevack Women of Har Shalom, Mazel Tov on Nancy's special birthday! Brandy & Steve Miller General Fund, In Memory of Sylvia Miller-Lawson Tracey & Jonathan Weiss HaBonim Donation, In Honor of June & Steve Volk Harriet & Jimmy Berg HaBonim Donation, In Honor of June & Steve Volk Glenn Dyer HaBonim Donation, In Honor of June & Steve Volk Barbara & Michael Horowitz HaBonim Donation, In Honor of June & Steve Volk Risa & Ted Offit HaBonim Donation, In Honor of June & Steve Volk Week of July 15 Annie & Tony Thomas General Donation, In Honor of Amy Baker Sara & Jonathan Klein General Donation, Wishing a quick and complete recovery to Bill Becker Hariet & Jimmy Berg General Donation, In Honor of Marcy Abelow's daughter Caroline's marriage to Jake Cohen Donna & Alan Stackman General Donation, In Honor of the birth of our grandchild Dylan Reid Stein, and his parents, Amanda & Greg Stein Liz & Neil Fellows Women of Har Shalom If you would like to make a donation In Honor of, In Celebration of, In Memory of You can do so easily via the button below Donate Here Sharing with the Community If you or know of a member of our community who has recently been recognized or honored, or if you have an event you wish to publicize to our community please contact us so we can share the news! ﻿ If you would like to make a donation in honor or in celebration of this news please click here. The WHS Judaica Shop is ﻿located in the Temple Lobby﻿ This Shabbat candle set would be a fabulous addition to your Shabbat table. Please contact Risa Offit or Audrey Seares to see this and many other stunning pieces for Passover. Property Watch & Management Handy Women & Repair Services Rina Slade 435-731-0617 Click Here to Visit Their Website Get Well Elaine Abramowitz, Terry Bohrer, Linda Brownstein, Martin Gelman, Laurie Hendin, Robyn Hendin Holzrichter, Ellen Kaplan, Karen Kravtin, Sage Lazar, Charles Lengle, Annette Lerman, Julie Lerman, David Lieberman, Kathy Lynch, Marcia Pace, Joy Rocklin, Joselyn Shoshana Bat Mazal Shloshim Selma Weintraub Ray Bishop ﻿Yahrzeits Honored this Week Alison Greenberg, Anna Guttenplan, Barbara De Leon, Caren Stanislaw, Charlie Pilzer, Ethel Henteleff, Eva Powell, George Streisfield, Hannah Rick, Harold Sidney Sussman, Jack Aronoff, Jay Julien, Jonathan Feinberg, Julian L Robinson, Lucy DeLeone, Michael Kagan, Patricia Hutchins, Richard Wong, Sol Oshinsky, Stephen Knepper, Yale Cohen Temple Har Shalom prays for the safe and swift release of all those being held captive in Israel and mourns those who have died.   Good Neighbors Volunteer Opportunity   THS in partnership with Good Neighbors is looking for volunteers to tutor adults who are learning English as a second language in Park City. To sign up, please visit the THS front desk or click this link Did you know the Good Neighbors Program helps ADULT English language learners or adults with limited literacy to develop skills and confidence to live and thrive in our community.   Did you know the Good Neighbors Program offers volunteer opportunities to tutor conversational English, English grammar, reading and/or writing. Volunteer opportunities are for mornings, afternoons, evenings or weekends. The time commitment is approximately 1-2 hours per week.   Did you know 43 million adults in the United States cannot read, write or do basic math above a third-grade level. Click here to visit Jennifer's website Hineinu (the Caring Committee), a partnership between Temple Har Shalom and Women of Har Shalom, enables the Temple Har Shalom community to support one another in their times of need and sorrow. Our services include organizing meal prep and delivery for those requiring help due to sickness, injury, or surgery, as well as providing meals for families observing Shiva. We are available to do errands or shop for homebound members as well as providing visits to those who are in need of company or just some cheering up. If you or someone you know in our THS community could benefit from our services, please contact Susan LaMere or Elissa Oshinsky. Click here to visit their website VOLUNTEERS WANTED We need your time and talent! For additional information on how to be involved and make a ﻿difference in our community, contact: Deb Sheldon THS Calendar Click here to view our calendar Administration office ﻿Is open by appointment only Click Here to Visit Their Website IJ & Jeanné Wagner Jewish Community Center Click this link to visit their website Jewish Family Service Get the Scoop on JFS! Date: Wednesday, August 28 Time: 3:00 to 4:30 PM Location: JFS Salt Lake Office (495 East 4500 South, Suite 100) Beat the summer heat with a refreshing ice cream treat from Rockwell Ice Cream Company at our Ice Cream Social & Open House!   Enjoy a tour, learn about our programs, and engage in conversations with our Executive Director, Board, and Staff. It's the perfect opportunity to catch up with JFS friends and make new ones!   This event is FREE, but please RSVP by August 23 to ensure we have enough cool treats for everyone. RSVP at http://weblink.donorperfect.com/IceCreamSocial2024 Jewish Family Service has three no cost virtual programs for people with dementia and their care partners 1-Caregiver Support Group. Meets every other Monday at 3 p.m. on Zoom*. · Contact Rosemary: rosemary@jfsutah.org, or call 801-746-4334 2-Dementia friendly activity day. Meets every other Thursday at 11 a.m. This is a fun, one hour, Zoom* activity for persons with dementia. For more information contact Rosemary: rosemary@jfsutah.org, or call ﻿801-746-4334 3-Gleeful Choir. This is a choir for persons living with dementia and their care partners. No experience necessary. Meets every Wednesday on Zoom* from 1-2:30. · For more information and registration: please contact: emily@musictherapyutah.com · www.gleefulchoir.com or call: 435-840-5661 *Zoom is the web platform that allows us to see each other on the computer, smart phone, or tablet screen. Our Zoom account is HIPPA compliant with the highest security available. Visit the JFS Website Here Hillel Delivers the Jewish Future Ninety four percent of the students impacted by Hillel say being Jewish will continue to be important to them after graduation. Who are your Rockstar leaders who we can directly connect to local and national Hillel leadership roles?  ﻿Who is involved in NFTY or BBYO?  Hillel International hopes that Juniors use the College Guide and other resources to make Jewish life on campus a primary factor in their college search, and for Seniors to view Hillel involvement as the next logical step in their Jewish journey.   Hillel delivers the Jewish future. . . Help us get Jewish teens excited to get involved with Hillel once they arrive on campuses! ﻿ Learn More about Hillel of Utah by visiting their website https://www.hillelutah.org/  Temple Har Shalom | 3700 N. Brookside Court | Park City, UT 84060 US Unsubscribe | Update Profile | Constant Contact Data Notice

Privacy Policy 


 



 Partner Offers: Partner offers subject to the terms and conditions of each individual offer. Partners and terms subject to change.






Mileage Offer on All US Rides: Lyft users who have Linked Accounts and selected ‘Delta SkyMiles’ as their travel partner in the Lyft app are eligible to receive 1 mile per $1 USD, excluding taxes, fees, tolls and tip spent on any Eligible Lyft Ride. “Eligible Lyft Ride” means any paid ride requested and completed using the Lyft app within the United States after registering the Linked Account. All Lyft ride types are eligible.


By submitting your information and registering for a Linked Account, , you agree to these terms and you agree that Lyft and Delta may share your information for the purpose of tracking and processing your miles into your SkyMiles account, sending you targeted offers, and sending your upcoming flight information to Lyft to coordinate Lyft rides with your Delta flights. Lyft and Delta may use anonymized, aggregated data for business analytics and offer optimization. SkyMiles account holder must be the same as the Lyft account holder in order to earn miles.

Additional Lyft terms: Tips not included. Restrictions apply. You may only earn points or miles through your Lyft account with one travel partner rewards program at a time. You can change which travel partner rewards program you want to activate at any time in the Lyft App. When Lyft fares are split among multiple users, only the user requesting the ride will earn miles. Lyft not available in all locations, including Puerto Rico. Lyft ride credits valid for Lyft users 18 years of age and older only. When calculating miles earned, fares will be rounded to the nearest dollar, and then any promotional offer will be calculated on the rounded amount. Lyft credits can only be used on the Lyft app
 and are not redeemable for cash. Lyft credits are non-transferable and cannot be combined with other offers. Any charges exceeding the Lyft credit value will be applied to your personal payment method. By using Lyft, you agree to Lyft’s 
https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=405f707bf624a2f2f23f139d7eadb4e5cacd42f6ef01fea70c2026f5e635332dfef7b2b6408cd264ee42b5e17a69f2930a69e92f6c57f3c6 
Terms of Service.  You may unlink your accounts at any time upon request by visiting the Lyft 
https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=405f707bf624a2f2087120eb3b8f3ff386a30d33485cdbcb54111411df7e063320f66787f95fdacc7d082d13348639151b67f9512c8c2a50 
Connected Services  page and scrolling down to "Connected Apps" and tapping "Revoke" next to Delta SkyMiles.


All SkyMiles program rules apply. To review the rules, please visit 
https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=405f707bf624a2f2ae061dc223b64f4116091ac76b60f0fb34a90729068e153f7210eb52ba68046766f4be24264063e562fb14401aae8ae5 
delta.com/memberguide.  Please allow up to 10 business days after completion of Eligible Lyft Ride for miles to be deposited in SkyMiles account. Partner offers subject to the terms and conditions of each individual offer. Offer(s) subject to change, and may be modified, suspended or terminated at any time without notice. Offers void where prohibited by law. Other restrictions may apply. Determinations of eligibility are made in Lyft and Delta’s sole discretion. Lyft and Delta reserve the right to review and investigate all offer activities, and to suspend accounts or remove rewards and miles for suspected abuse, violation of terms, or fraud.


Delta, SkyMiles, and the Delta logo are registered service marks of Delta Air Lines, Inc.






Starbucks Terms & Conditions 


 Terms apply. Starbucks Rewards and Delta SkyMiles benefits are available at participating stores. For full details, visit 
https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=405f707bf624a2f28347244ab5362cb4c9c35a40276142916cae1e7bc3ebb782fee320760f710ac489749446af218928f031acb552118cce 
deltastarbucks.com/terms. 

Double Stars on Delta Travel Days: On Delta Travel Days, earn (a) 4 Stars for every $1 USD (excluding dollars spent toward taxes and tips) spent on Qualifying Purchases when paying with a registered Starbucks Card, and (b) 2 Stars per $1 USD (excluding dollars spent toward taxes and tips) when you scan the barcode in the Starbucks app prior to checkout, then pay with credit, debit or cash. “Qualifying Purchase” means any purchase at a participating Starbucks Store of food, beverage(s), and/or merchandise, excluding purchases of alcoholic beverages, Starbucks Cards and Starbucks Card reloads. “Delta Travel Days” means the period starting 12:01AM and ending at Midnight on the local departure
 date of a Delta-marketed or Delta-operated flight for which you include your SkyMiles number in the itinerary; provided that if there are multiple connecting segments with a layover in such itinerary, or if the itinerary continues beyond a single 24-hour period, then the Delta Travel Day will be deemed to be the departure date of the of the first segment of the itinerary. Delta flights booked within 48 hours prior to the time of the scheduled departure will not be deemed to be a Delta Travel Day and you will not be eligible to earn this Double Stars offer under such itinerary.

Mileage Offer on Starbucks purchases: Earn 1 mile per $1 USD (excluding dollars spent toward taxes and tips) of Qualifying Purchases. “Qualifying Purchase” means any purchase at a participating Starbucks Store of food, beverage(s), and/or merchandise, excluding purchases of alcoholic beverages, Starbucks Cards and Starbucks Card reloads. Must pay using a linked payment method or Starbucks Card registered to your Starbucks Rewards account, or by scanning the barcode in the Starbucks app prior to checkout.

subject: Asianometry on Japan's Chemical Dominance, Stratechery Updates, and Chinese New Year Schedule Changes (Stratechery Update 1-29-2025)
body_text:
Chinese New Year wishes, and a scheduling adjustment.

View in browser ( https://stratechery.com/2025/happy-chinese-new-year-update-tomorrow/?access_token=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsImtpZCI6InN0cmF0ZWNoZXJ5LnBhc3Nwb3J0Lm9ubGluZSIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..Lq81SoSJFw2u1K7XXceCzdpt5y6PgYqNET65Ul8mmBq3tCtPNdY8G06YtKdVnlwkPAVn_mX8hgivAn3tc3RCpZpOtmuIvEH3yF4yC3je0faUHSfSMxBIRnbGcxwrB3oLq3MD36bpH0hUtFw2mcXQTWbF1zEAFEaRiap4QPLvWr9g2MSoWjnfbdi2UuNP48lUdc8jHQ3ETd2m9_NRF0zxuDQ6umh01w3tGBCO1aoI-XdPFcpe2V0OHejmzWdbq6YwfcIsa2YalFYGi5fmz3KoQtMmooIwIv3q4irBFsABwR81NwucdYnRygMjcf8Yh9slv-8e6VZhc61oj1yIbXSmTg )

( https://stratechery.com )

*****************************************************************************************************
Asianometry on Japan's Chemical Dominance, Stratechery Updates, and Chinese New Year Schedule Changes
*****************************************************************************************************

( https://stratechery.com/2025/happy-chinese-new-year-update-tomorrow/?access_token=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsImtpZCI6InN0cmF0ZWNoZXJ5LnBhc3Nwb3J0Lm9ubGluZSIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..Lq81SoSJFw2u1K7XXceCzdpt5y6PgYqNET65Ul8mmBq3tCtPNdY8G06YtKdVnlwkPAVn_mX8hgivAn3tc3RCpZpOtmuIvEH3yF4yC3je0faUHSfSMxBIRnbGcxwrB3oLq3MD36bpH0hUtFw2mcXQTWbF1zEAFEaRiap4QPLvWr9g2MSoWjnfbdi2UuNP48lUdc8jHQ3ETd2m9_NRF0zxuDQ6umh01w3tGBCO1aoI-XdPFcpe2V0OHejmzWdbq6YwfcIsa2YalFYGi5fmz3KoQtMmooIwIv3q4irBFsABwR81NwucdYnRygMjcf8Yh9slv-8e6VZhc61oj1yIbXSmTg ) Wednesday, January 29, 2025

Listen to this Update in your podcast player ( https://stratechery.passport.online/member/podcast?url=https%3A%2F%2Frss.stratechery.passport.online%2Ffeed%2Fpodcast%2FwW4bN7siMzR7s6ypDygt8 )

Good morning,

Today is Chinese New Year — 新年快樂！— and while this was originally scheduled to be a day off, there are a host of Big Tech earnings coming out today and tomorrow. I'm particularly interested in management commentary given the news of the last week, so I'm making a (very rare!) scheduling adjustment: tomorrow I will publish an Update likely covering Meta and/or Microsoft, and then publish the weekly Stratechery Interview on Friday.

Second, I wanted to apologize again for anyone that had issues last week with the updates to Stratechery. This was, as you might have surmised, a pretty massive update that extended far beyond the website redesign, including significant database changes to Passport. That part actually went fine — the latest version has been running on SemiAnalysis ( https://www.semianalysis.com/ ) for a couple of months — but we did run into several interlocking issues that were related to previous migrations. There were also a few bugs that only manifested at Stratechery scale, including some CDN issues, some issues related to how the Stratechery bundle works, and maddeningly, a third party library update to our UUID encoding library that dropped characters for IDs that started 00. It was very much a "when it rains it pours" situation.

Regardless, I did not deliver the user experience that I want to, and I'm sorry.

What is particularly disappointing is that these bugs may have overshadowed the biggest news surrounding the upgrade, which was the addition of the Asianometry podcast and newsletter to the Stratechery Plus bundle. Please do check out my introductory post ( https://stratechery.com/2025/stratechery-plus-asianometry/?access_token=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsImtpZCI6InN0cmF0ZWNoZXJ5LnBhc3Nwb3J0Lm9ubGluZSIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..SBkuNkbWHlM5jhEbehQ-B6ejcThngeo-9BWdccLPyjB15nrS1_iTwi4Fh-keCwAY3suS_pz-gxMz2dtB2wMYVpKPGNVLYwqbZ6UMR5l5IsLTgn1tF-ZMj3C-eYG1lUCfXPneiIp8X_RgTr16uX7gKx04j08Te0dI9gHJs6EYh4JGEkQsbMHGl77B66-gsoqVW53uXyiAQyHv0Y0y2qgjY_VYImXbtUyWGLSq7lr2dVQzLNeZtUPlcbLx1L-F8qiqSpSiJ3XclUx2bLY0Eaf63OVpw_bbI5ybxAgDpxuEstVz7ShXmVTkprihx8rkopWwBklcmfpergJE2sRtlwbbpQ ) , if you missed it, as well as the Interview I did with Jon Yu ( https://stratechery.com/2025/an-interview-with-jon-yu-about-youtube-and-making-semiconductors/?access_token=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsImtpZCI6InN0cmF0ZWNoZXJ5LnBhc3Nwb3J0Lm9ubGluZSIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..KjPcbn0ZSh4qANqVqbekvbBltxnGeT9VDmWxW3OVNSgvPQsOkTia5PEYIbAVH62_txQ6VGX-Q4xzZpVZwWP1IrM5wRTrCxpdHTzion2HTH1A6y9ivpHfrzcXU-Fmjq6sLRcW3nTsgNfvnJi9ffgWyxr-_FtekK_Y3pi4BRHQdA6VaZuHAMCI4FbiExde2sKZPsRmBkgLHQwRnH4QWk-MQ4Khhs_v0BLzyUG7h7a_z30w-NhG7gDKAQGCJlXIUhQxwjDuC8BjOTk2Hr3xMffdrIFbGH3Lk6EK_cg9D7JLPlsMVqjo8UtvUQte3KrPm9WcL72k1-j2M-frQiqCgXoJQQ ). One of the most interesting aspects of that Interview was our discussion about Japan's dominance of many of the chemicals involved in manufacturing semiconductors, and Jon just came out with a new YouTube video about exactly that:

( https://www.youtube.com/embed/3T4FpRm2uwk?si=aygDertrBnRBXlyv )

To listen to this video as a podcast, you can click "Add Asianometry" at the bottom of this email; to read the transcript visit the Asianometry Passport site ( https://asianometry.passport.online/member/episode/japans-semiconductor-photoresist-monopoly ) , and while you're there visit your account page ( https://asianometry.passport.online/member ) to receive transcripts by email.

I'm thrilled to have Jon on board, and wish him and you a happy year of the snake! See you tomorrow.

Listen to this update and other Stratechery Plus content in your podcast player:
Stratechery ( https://stratechery.passport.online/member/podcast?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstratechery.passport.online%2Ffeed%2Fpodcast%2FwW4bN7siMzR7s6ypDygt8 ) | Sharp Tech ( https://sharptech.fm/member/podcast?url=https%3A%2F%2Fsharptech.fm%2Ffeed%2Fpodcast%2FwW4bN7siMzR7s6ypDygt8 ) | Dithering ( https://dithering.passport.online/member/podcast?url=https%3A%2F%2Fdithering.passport.online%2Ffeed%2Fpodcast%2FwW4bN7siMzR7s6ypDygt8 ) | Sharp China ( https://sharpchina.fm/member/podcast?url=https%3A%2F%2Fsharpchina.fm%2Ffeed%2Fpodcast%2FwW4bN7siMzR7s6ypDygt8 )
Greatest Of All Talk ( https://goat.passport.online/member/podcast?url=https%3A%2F%2Fgoat.passport.online%2Ffeed%2Fpodcast%2FwW4bN7siMzR7s6ypDygt8 ) | Asianometry ( https://asianometry.passport.online/member/podcast?url=https%3A%2F%2Fasianometry.passport.online%2Ffeed%2Fpodcast%2FwW4bN7siMzR7s6ypDygt8 )

subject: Re: [EXTERNAL] Re: DEV23-3374 (ADU @ 316 Costello Drive) - Resubmittal Status
body_text:
Hi Ed,

Vimbai from Lea & Braze addressed your comments and plans were uploaded
again to the portal this past Friday, 1/31. Please note that she is happy
to hop on a call with you to discuss any further questions or comments -
she can be reached at the following phone number and email:

Email: vanda@leabraze.com*|* Main: 510-887-4086 x183 |Direct: 408-516-3782

Please let me know when you will be able to look at the additional
information that's been uploaded and the status of the permit approval.

Thank you,

Rebecca Dupree

> *From: *Duazo, Ed <Ed.Duazo@PLN.SCCGOV.ORG>
> *Date: *Thursday, August 8, 2024 at 10:56 AM
> *To: *Vimbainashe Anda <vanda@leabraze.com>
> *Cc: *Robert Medan <robert@medanarch.com>, Scott Silver <
> scottmsilver@gmail.com>, Rosas, Cristina <cristina.rosas@pln.sccgov.org>
> *Subject: *DEV23-3374 (ADU @ 316 Costello Drive) - Resubmittal Status
>
> Hi Vimbai,
>
> I spoke to Robert Medan this morning regarding the status of the ADU
> application for 316 Costello Drive (County Record No. DEV23-3374). In
> reviewing the status of the application, it looks like there are a few
> issues.
>
>
>
>    1. The current status in the system is “pending resubmittal.” Though
>    uploads were made on 7/25 and 7/30, the Permit Center provided comments
>    that needed to be addressed before the resubmittal could be routed out for
>    review. The comment/feedback from the Permit Center on 7/31 provides
>    instruction to, “Please assemble the plans in one PDF, ensuring the plan
>    sheets are all the same size.” In discussing the uploads with Robert, it
>    seems like some of the plan sheets may have been embedded in the hydrology
>    report as a single .pdf. If plan sheets that are to be included in the
>    building set are embedded in the report, they need to be pulled out,
>    inserted into the building plans, and the full building set submitted.
>
>
>
>    2. Based on a cursory review of the hydrology report, a drainage
>    permit is required for the project. The drainage permit requires a separate
>    permit application. Please submit a separate application for the drainage
>    permit. Additional information on drainage submittal requirements is
>    available here
>    <https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__plandev.sccgov.org_how_apply-2Dpermit_drainage-2Dpermit&d=DwMGaQ&c=euGZstcaTDllvimEN8b7jXrwqOf-v5A_CdpgnVfiiMM&r=XQBPOax5RqBV9s_VLSh4QzgApz6gyXfLbMFoFRNW3sM&m=5O7O8eVeVF8Y_0VfZixvJ5qfaaUG0g2jBaE_vGgem7Dmj5NB5yCeIRNnk9huFoCK&s=kv1WHe0loEL2GE7zqSkMgGjvhq_0NOVyyw4YFqZw2B8&e=>
>    .
>
>
>
> Once the comment from the Permit Center has been addressed, the ADU
> resubmittal can be routed for review. The Department of Environmental
> Health, Land Development Engineering (LDE), and Roads & Airports still need
> to approve the ADU permit application.
>
>
>
> Please note that LDE clearance will not be issued until the associated
> drainage permit is approved and ready to issue (the ADU building permit and
> the drainage permit should be issued in tandem).
>
>
>
> If you have questions, please let me know.
>
> Thanks.
> Ed
>
>

subject: 🚨 Campus Crisis Alert
body_text:
— 
https://view.adlmail.org/?qs=1117d536cb0600ccf38b4edd6feaf202e15386f67b58063b6988183e864e16add1369c76e4aedfdc58d8507816e698380bed7318f8997d6ed087ce1be030d79e6bd8b3c05d4dcd180f6404ddcfb61fa1 
View email in browser  — 

https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f73bd4f77ccd97e1411349c0383a1c7054670ae2acc540708ccc1cc749959458a0b7b5fdff7a61b70d57ea05065e0e8fa5 



 


 
Good morning. The Campus Crisis Alert and all the work that ADL does to fight antisemitism and other forms of hate wouldn’t be possible without you. Will you help keep Jewish people and other targets of hate safe every month? Start a monthly gift before January 31 and your first two gifts will be 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f7d0798ce4d3c4f4ad5b4d7a32fd12ff01ac2239f07a6131bd892c22da57db65825c5977cc1d4c8fe42b116b05a4eccc27 
matched for 2x the impact. 

https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f7d0798ce4d3c4f4ad5b4d7a32fd12ff01ac2239f07a6131bd892c22da57db65825c5977cc1d4c8fe42b116b05a4eccc27 
Yes, I Want to Have an Impact Every Month 

1. 📰 Top Stories

Maine. The University of Maine System’s board of trustees 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f7cf85abc7a8a354189926cfecde1779a4fb625121cae16a3e5f5a6274337bcff1cbe285fcbd9da064716578bbc3d0dea9 
rejected  a proposal to divest from Israel during a special Dec. 17 online meeting, despite pressure from the campus chapter of Jewish Voice for Peace (JVP). Trustee Trish Riley emphasized the importance of fostering dialogue, stating, “To take a position on your demands would effectively shut down opportunities for conversation… and in doing so, undermine our educational mission.” The JVP chapter, comprising roughly 10 members, expressed disappointment, declaring on Instagram that they will “continue to amplify demands in solidarity with Palestinians” and pursue divestment efforts.


 ~~~~~

Colorado. Two students who had been disciplined by the University of Colorado-Boulder for their roles in an 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f75e589b96bb9ce8488c3a2789447973e043f0616507028a0a0f91e85878af924c588ada660aad18292c2786a611986a2a 
anti-Israel protest  on campus are now suing the school, claiming that their rights were violated. The Oct. 3 divestment-oriented protest was organized by Students for Justice in Palestine to disrupt a career fair hosting military and defense contractors on campus. SJP was also put in “bad standing” with the university as a result of the protest and other acts that violated school policies. The students named in the lawsuit were banned from campus for nearly two months after participating in the protest.

(Source: Boulder_SJP | Instagram)


 ~~~~~

Lehigh. This week the U.S. Education Department 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f77b1880c096d233225304e99cc9e566da09e14d9d3e40d6ff3880b9713d2bc7b6fb835cc22fae98a5bbb2f2afb3c55d99 
announced a resolution  to its case with Lehigh University, noting “concerns that the university appears not to have evaluated whether individual incidents of alleged harassment … contributed to a hostile environment for students on campus.” Specific examples 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f74640e9371e565f71dcf29fa2981b8fc3c82e1be5dbe2443503e6fc8de9f4f369d5ff07f3395c24b0708089c8676136c4 
in the report  include multiple instances of mezuzahs being stolen from the doors of dorm rooms. The agreement with the school does not constitute an admission of liability or wrongdoing by Lehigh, but the school has in any case committed to steps that include holding trainings and conducting a “climate assessment.”


 ~~~~~

Florida. The latest Hate Crimes in Florida Report showed a 94% increase in antisemitic hate crimes in the state from 2022 to 2023. Ashley Moody, Florida’s AG, announced a 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f7fbe93fc569e535063a8674983070617c98b4ed146c66caf975a2feb522b0d7a9dbbd711387135492d01d6bcb59921d7c 
law enforcement training session  at Florida Atlantic University in April. Developed in partnership with the Combat Antisemitism Movement and George Washington University’s Program on Extremism, the training will focus on the presence of Hamas and its imagery at protests on American campuses. The training will also cover the roles of Iranian and Hezbollah networks in propaganda. "Florida is a law-and-order state, and we have taken extraordinary steps to protect the Jewish community since the October 7th attacks," Moody said.

2. 🏆 Campus Champions

Bright Lights. The Shine A Light on Antisemitism 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f7787bc8bb4d50382c83729c0f6770562af48fa98b7607d99a8fd935c44be279fa05efd1f75c3422159aabb4efd46b6ebf 
Civic Courage Award , powered by 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f73e92a45c6443f25dd0dafc6069fa49f951b4825486a64e0e0b8e613551bd3f5e81734660c1e428426eb3bb529fe6b61e 
The Jewish Education Project,  honors individuals combating antisemitism through leadership and action. This year’s university student winners included Abby Mehr (The Ohio State University), Olivia Raykhman (West Point), and Emma Steinhause (University of Maryland). Their efforts highlight extraordinary courage and commitment to countering hate. The award was also given to thirteen other individuals, including four university professors. Among them is Associate Dean Luis Inoa of Vassar College, recognized for his exceptional commitment to fostering inclusion for Jewish and Zionist students.


 ~~~~~

Academia vs. Antisemitism. The recent appearance of U.N. Special Rapporteur Francesca Albanese at the London School of Economics underscores a troubling reality: universities are failing to protect Jewish students from hostility masquerading as academic discourse. Known for controversial statements, including claims of “Jewish lobby” influence over America, Albanese’s presence raised alarms among Jewish students and communities. Her rhetoric, coupled with recent incidents such as attacks on pro-Israel displays at Exeter University and threats at King’s College London, showcases a dangerous environment for Jewish students. Her presence, as author Fair Shukurlu 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f76bc311b503bfc9afb873a8c528fb7e85b88092a7ed246fb3a1729e0cd1fba012293925931af1ffc1d8f48a8ae846c26d 
notes , sends a dangerous message, fueling an environment where Jewish students face threats.


 3. 💪✡️ Am Yisrael Chai

From The Battleground to the Boardroom. Itamar Ben Hemo, co-founder of Israeli data integration platform Rivery, 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f75f0af63682f460c7954d86f0d9bf1cb6c91d440dd6f2e58c48a93e0adf3d84cf8b86a36de7b8229261b96fc131a9e49a 
exemplifies resilience.  Nearly a year after being critically injured while serving in the IDF reserves, Ben Hemo led his company to a $100 million acquisition by Boomi, a US-based data management firm. Despite facing life-threatening injuries in a Hamas ambush and undergoing months of recovery, Ben Hemo remained committed to his company, conducting meetings even from his hospital bed. His return to Rivery brought stability, paving the way for the acquisition. The deal reflects Boomi’s trust in Israeli tech, and a portion of the proceeds will support the family of an employee lost to war.

(Courtesy: Rivery)


 ~~~~~

Drawing Closer. As the Assad regime faltered, the Druze community in the Golan Heights has increasingly grown 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f726d49730e95c9996194827d2450b7f6eea58f0db1f9a79ba5b6b14792ac19c17738a06c6bf8b644b68ee3755a1b08ed1 
closer to Israel,  as is shown by the doubling of the percentage of Golan Druze who now hold Israeli citizenship. As researcher Dr. Yusri Hazran explained, “The Druze see their mother country disintegrating with the fall of the Assad regime and they are looking for an anchor.” Factors encouraging this momentum towards citizenship include the anger caused by the rocket attack by Hezbollah (an Assad ally) last year that killed 12 Druze children, and the gradual increase in economic ties to Israel based on tourism and construction.

DEEPER: Late last year, ADL's Israel office launched an important new initiative — the 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f70f7e0a6fe6cf67e3d95bc6d7b2dc52d7249e60e6446603e0af20ed7962910b62ec6395fc8efed18e47446c9aadb14d07 
Druze Leadership Program , with a goal of promoting a just and cohesive Israel.

4. 📣 Info and Action: 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f7ef390c15c68fe1f9fb93452322d44f897101f381d7ec877e1f278bc59e6cf900816ed16d198fd06d92d2e6cd0e39c285 
Not on My Campus 

	- 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f7419f84b6d1a619d98ac8403f6d1127a5c223fbde56b739713ba1d91493a608a9de2fea8a4a4a261887273c4bd8d84a45 
Students  — how to take action, deal with antisemitism, and organize your community.
	- 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f73eb06c566a0914784daf3012c7850692391804c40e505654c9746e24f255d655775cbe28dec415d55d763f0de4cee71f 
Alumni  — how to organize a sign-on letter, answer a fundraising request, or write a letter to a university president.
	- 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f7642f8cc74c1a53c9b2d29619a8a117446968eca6688421eafcecfd089d52f5540ea7e9f6d2a38373ba9334032b3396c7 
Parents  — write a letter about commencement, organize a dial day, or get help selecting a college for your student.
	- 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f7e4d0ba9006c306ab134f87a75873c080d482310bcf14c31808ca00b907c667077452266c03dad5dd9cf04590aadf04bc 
Faculty and Staff  — support for impacted faculty and staff, and guidance on how to discuss what constitutes antisemitism and anti-Zionism, and how to provide help to students and colleagues.
	- 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f7e8ad18e4ca9b9b2e1d881fd7939155a6db18f2d67fe8d82f620d1ca022d9c2e2eb31cdee1ffc388a25c117cfbaacd08e 
Glossary  of Commonly Used Antisemitic Phrases Heard at Protests.
	- 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f7cb7d84a75413d6993a4e898b264eb72c3bf55eb5a359d3dc05c3f3c363db3da8cd0e9ad2bf4ec782fa6d00c74841243c 
Campus Antisemitism Report Card  — see the grade a college earned in this first-ever report card.
	- 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f7c5dd071f0772e249900591a84f0ec339a6090630c8b2620e584c80192c59bc0e41a7cfe0f4e3d91ce2d7d64ea872d17d 
Report an Antisemitic Incident. 
	- 
 Are you a student or know one who needs legal help? Contact our 
https://click.adlmail.org/?qs=7b22fc00d72d32f7fac40a2edb69365a8e90198fb35892e3068dd7261902e1a13f4369b90d0ad82e40127ce4c32993e60a184fa8b23249f3 
Campus Antisemitism Legal Line  (CALL), run by ADL, Hillel, the Brandeis Center, and Gibson Dunn. Hundreds of incidents have been reported. You are not alone!

*If your booking was made at least 7 days in advance:* You have 24 hours
from the time booking was made to cancel your flight and receive a full
refund to your original form of payment, without a cancellation fee.

*JetBlue Change/Cancel Policy:* There is no fee to change (or cancel) Blue,
Blue Extra, Blue Plus or Mint fares. Blue Basic fares cannot be changed and
are subject to a cancel fee of $100 for travel wholly within the U.S.,
Caribbean, Mexico or Central America, or $200 for all other routes.

Fare difference may apply and funds may be in the form of a JetBlue travel
credit, valid for 12 months from original ticketing date. Same-day switches
may be made without a fare difference for $75 (excludes Blue Basic fares,
unless booked on or after March 18 for travel on or before Jun 17, 2024). Click
here
